companies.name: Taste Labs
companies.description: Building the AI powered future of fandom where fans create, shape and share the worlds they love.
companies.lead_investor: CRV
companies.score_rationale: Creative/producer founders, no big-tech/research pedigree — below bar

companies.name: Tolan
companies.description:
Portola is a San Francisco-based AI startup founded in mid-2023 by Quinten Farmer, Evan Goldschmidt, and Ajay Mehta. The company initially focused on creating tools for children, such as image generators and story builders, before pivoting to develop Tolan, an AI companionship app featuring animated alien characters from "Planet Portola." 

Tolan, launched in February 2025, serves as an "Embodied Companion" that engages users through natural voice and touch interactions. The app personalizes experiences by matching users with AI companions based on a personality interview. Tolan aims to provide support and inspiration, helping users navigate daily decisions and personal growth. The app has seen significant success, achieving over 3 million downloads and rapid revenue growth shortly after its launch. Portola employs a diverse team of about 12 people, including engineers, researchers, and creatives.
companies.lead_investor: Khosla Ventures
companies.score_rationale: Repeat founders (Quinten Farmer, ex-Even) + Khosla; strong-not-elite, no big-tech/research

companies.name: Raindrop
companies.description:
Raindrop Agency is a full-service marketing agency located in San Diego, California. The agency specializes in brand development, creative production, and performance marketing, focusing on creating engaging marketing strategies for challenger brands in the retail, direct-to-consumer, and tech sectors. Founded by CEO Jacques Spitzer and COO Adam Wagner, Raindrop has evolved from its early focus on real estate to becoming a national player, notably scaling Dr. Squatch from $3 million to over $150 million in revenue.

Raindrop offers a comprehensive range of services, including brand strategy, creative production, digital marketing, and full-service campaign execution. The agency prides itself on producing all content in-house, ensuring quick turnaround times and a unique approach to advertising. Their work has garnered numerous accolades, including multiple Emmy Awards and Super Bowl ads. Raindrop also publishes The Founders’ Report, providing insights and strategies to help clients navigate the marketing landscape effectively.
companies.lead_investor: Lightspeed
companies.score_rationale: Marketing-agency CEO, no big-tech/research pedigree — below bar

companies.name: Exa
companies.description:
Exa is an AI-optimized search engine and applied AI lab founded in 2021. The company focuses on providing search infrastructure tailored for artificial intelligence applications. Exa has raised $85 million in Series B funding, achieving a valuation of $700 million. Its innovative approach utilizes neural network-based search technology, allowing for a deeper understanding of context and user intent, which results in more relevant search outcomes.

Exa offers a range of products, including a Search API for developers, Websets for comprehensive entity retrieval, and a Website Crawler for large-scale web content processing. The platform features natural language processing, flexible filtering options, and zero data retention for enterprise clients. Exa serves a diverse clientele, including AI startups, consulting firms, and academic researchers, all benefiting from its advanced search capabilities. The company's vision is to achieve "perfect search" by organizing information for optimal AI access, with plans for future expansion in indexing and processing systems.
companies.lead_investor: Andreessen Horowitz

companies.name: Browserbase
companies.description:
Browserbase is a technology company based in San Francisco, founded in 2024 by Paul Klein IV. The company specializes in cloud-based browser infrastructure tailored for AI agents and automated web interactions. Currently at the Series B stage, Browserbase has raised around $67.4 million, including a $40 million Series B round in 2025, with support from investors like Kleiner Perkins and Okta Ventures.

The company offers serverless, scalable headless browsers accessible via API, allowing developers to run thousands of browsers simultaneously for tasks such as web automation and data extraction. Key products include Stagehand, an open-source framework for building web agents, and Director, a tool for human-like AI interactions. Browserbase emphasizes performance and security, featuring globally distributed browsers, compliance with SOC-2 Type 1 and HIPAA, and capabilities like automatic CAPTCHA solving and proxy optimization. The platform supports various integrations and provides services for hosting and managing headless browsers, enabling reliable automation in secure environments.
companies.lead_investor: Kleiner Perkins, CRV, Notable Capital

companies.name: Sierra
companies.description:
Sierra is a conversational AI platform founded in 2023 that helps businesses create, deploy, and manage personalized AI agents for customer service and support. The platform focuses on delivering human-like conversations and action-oriented capabilities, ensuring alignment with brand identity. Sierra's headquarters is in San Francisco, with additional offices in New York, Atlanta, and London. The company has raised $285 million in funding, reflecting strong investor confidence in its approach to enterprise AI.

The core offering, AgentOS, allows businesses to build and manage AI agents that integrate seamlessly with existing enterprise systems. Key features include the Experience Manager for designing branded conversations, multi-model AI integration for reliability, and personalized agents capable of handling various customer interactions across multiple channels. Sierra also emphasizes security and compliance, ensuring data privacy and strict access controls. The platform supports 24/7 multilingual assistance and aims to enhance customer experience through continuous improvement and analytics.
companies.lead_investor: Tiger Global, GV
companies.score_rationale: Clay Bavor co-founder — ex-Google VP (Labs/AR/VR) — clears bar (a); AI agents, Bret Taylor co

companies.name: TwelveLabs
companies.description:
Twelve Labs is an AI company based in San Francisco, California, with an additional office in Seoul, South Korea. Founded in 2021, the company specializes in video intelligence, developing advanced foundation models that enable machines to understand video content similarly to humans. Their self-serve SaaS platform processes video as a multidimensional "volume," utilizing multimodal AI models to analyze audio, visuals, movement, emotions, and context over time.

The platform offers features such as video analysis and search, allowing users to upload videos for easy retrieval without manual tagging. It also supports content generation and embedding, automating workflows for various applications across media, security, automotive, retail, entertainment, sports, gaming, healthcare, and financial services. Twelve Labs has raised $107 million in funding and is guided by a team of industry experts and advisors, emphasizing genuine comprehension in video AI to enhance human-machine interaction.
companies.score_rationale: Multimodal video research team, real ML focus but founder pedigree unclear on card; single-modality

companies.name: Greptile
companies.description:
Greptile is an AI-powered codebase expert that helps developers and software teams understand, search, and review large codebases using natural language. Founded in 2023 and based in San Francisco, California, Greptile offers a codebase-optimized Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) platform.

The platform includes a web application and chatbot that allows developers to connect to various repositories and ask questions in plain English, receiving accurate code references and explanations. Additionally, Greptile provides an API for teams to integrate AI expertise into their development workflows, supporting automated pull request reviews, bug diagnosis, and documentation updates. Key features include automated PR reviews, in-line comments for identifying issues, and custom context for enforcing coding standards.

Greptile reviews millions of lines of code weekly, helping to surface numerous bugs that might be missed during manual reviews. The company serves over 250 clients, including notable names like Stripe and Amazon, and has raised significant funding to support its growth.
companies.lead_investor: Benchmark, Initialized Capital, Y Combinator, Cory Levy
companies.score_rationale: Benchmark-led but founders show no big-tech/lab pedigree on card

companies.name: Factory
companies.description:
Factory is an AI-powered platform that enhances software engineering through "agent-native software development." Founded in 2023, it utilizes specialized autonomous AI agents called "Droids" to manage various tasks such as coding, testing, debugging, documentation, incident response, and project management. The platform integrates seamlessly into existing developer workflows without requiring changes to tools or processes.

The Droids include the Knowledge Droid for engineering research and documentation, the Code Droid for generating code changes, the Reliability Droid for incident investigation and debugging, and the Product Droid for project management and feature planning. Factory supports a wide range of development tools and offers enterprise features, ensuring robust security and compliance. It is designed for both startups and enterprises, allowing developers to automate their workflows effectively. Factory is backed by notable investors and trusted by teams at Groq for accelerating product launches.
companies.lead_investor: Blackstone, Insight Partners, Sequoia, NEA, Khosla Ventures, The Twenty Minute VC, MANTIS, Evantic Capital

companies.name: Resolve AI
companies.description:
Resolve AI is an AI startup founded by former Splunk executives Spiros Xanthos and Mayank Agarwal. The company focuses on developing autonomous Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) tools designed to automate production system maintenance, incident management, and software engineering tasks. Resolve AI aims to significantly reduce the time it takes to resolve incidents and alerts, allowing engineers to concentrate on feature development.

The core offering is the AI Production Engineer, an advanced system that autonomously triages alerts, investigates incidents, and performs root-cause analysis. It integrates with tools like AWS, Kubernetes, GitHub, and Slack, providing capabilities such as incident resolution, debugging, cost optimization, and development assistance. Resolve AI has achieved approximately $4 million in annual recurring revenue and has received significant funding, including a $35 million seed round and a Series A round, valuing the company at $1 billion. The company fosters a collaborative team culture in San Francisco and serves industries such as financial services, healthcare, and media, with Coinbase as a notable customer.
companies.lead_investor: Greylock Partners, Lightspeed Venture Partners, Unusual Ventures, Artisanal Ventures
companies.score_rationale: Spiros Xanthos prior founder (Omnition/Sysdig observability), strong serial; Lightspeed-led agents

companies.name: Mastra
companies.description:
Mastra is an open-source TypeScript framework designed for building, deploying, and managing AI-powered applications and agents. Founded in 2024 by Sam, Shane, and Abhi, the company is based in San Francisco and has a team of 20 people. Mastra has raised $13 million in funding from notable venture firms, including Gradient, basecase, and Y Combinator.

The framework offers a suite of tools that support the entire lifecycle of AI agent development. Key features include the ability to create autonomous agents that utilize large language models, orchestrate complex workflows, and connect to over 40 LLM providers. Mastra also provides capabilities for data integration, cloud deployment, and performance tracking, making it suitable for various applications such as conversational agents, workflow automation, and decision-support tools. The company serves a range of technology clients, including notable names like Fireworks, Vetnio, and Medusa.
companies.lead_investor: Spark Capital, Gokul Rajaram, Gradient Ventures
companies.score_rationale: Sam Bhagwat ex-Gatsby; agent-framework authors, builder pedigree but not big-tech-senior/research

companies.name: Applied Compute
companies.description:
Applied Compute is an AI startup founded by former OpenAI researchers, specializing in creating Specific Intelligence for enterprises. The company focuses on unlocking a company's latent knowledge to train custom AI models and deploy in-house AI agent workforces. With $80 million in funding from notable investors, Applied Compute aims to enhance productivity and efficiency through specialized AI agents tailored to meet the unique needs of each organization.

The core platform enables organizations to harness their internal knowledge, train dedicated AI agents, and integrate them seamlessly with existing teams. These agents are designed to operate under company ownership and control, continuously learning within specific domains. By embedding engineers directly with client teams, Applied Compute ensures rapid deployment and top-tier performance, significantly reducing the time required to achieve results. Early adopters of the platform include Cognition, DoorDash, and Mercor, who leverage the technology to drive measurable business outcomes.
companies.lead_investor: Kleiner Perkins

companies.name: Conductor
companies.description:
Conductor is a data platform for the grueling challenges that vertical SaaS startups face integrating data from non-tech industries (e.g., logistics, healthcare, food, and construction).

Integrating with these non-tech industries requires tedious data management and painstakingly building dozens of bespoke pipelines for numerous fragile sources and disparate structures. It's a huge pain. Teams significantly underestimate these obstacles, underinvest in their internal tooling, and consequently cannot scale their product. We aim to turn these startups' data challenges into their competitive advantage.
companies.lead_investor: Matrix Partners
companies.score_rationale: Long-time marketing-SaaS CEO, no big-tech/research pedigree

companies.name: Sentry
companies.description:
Sentry is a software company that specializes in developer-focused error monitoring, performance tracking, and debugging tools. Founded in 2012 in San Francisco, it originated as an open-source project aimed at helping developers track application errors. Sentry has since expanded its support to over 100 programming languages and platforms, serving more than 100,000 organizations globally.

The company's core platform provides error tracking and exception handling, featuring real-time alerting, performance monitoring, and integrations into developer workflows. Sentry emphasizes delivering critical issue context to facilitate quick resolutions, making it a valuable tool for developers. With a mission to simplify debugging, Sentry processes billions of exceptions daily and supports both self-hosted and cloud options. Its customer base includes notable organizations like Dropbox, GitHub, and Atlassian, highlighting its significance in the software development landscape.
companies.lead_investor: NEA, Accel, K5 Global, Bond

companies.name: LlamaIndex
companies.description:
LlamaIndex is an open-source data framework and developer-first agent platform based in San Francisco, CA. Founded in 2022, it connects large language models (LLMs) with external data sources to create retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) applications and AI workflows. The platform has gained significant traction, with over 4 million monthly package downloads and more than 200,000 users on LlamaCloud.

The company offers a comprehensive ecosystem for LLM applications, including an open-source framework with tools for data ingestion, structuring, and querying. LlamaCloud provides a cloud-based platform for document automation, while LlamaParse is an API for transforming unstructured data into RAG-ready formats. LlamaIndex also features a modular agent framework for context-aware automation, collaborating with Nvidia on blueprints for research and writing agents. The company serves major enterprises like Rakuten Group, The Carlyle Group, and Salesforce, supporting complex RAG pipelines and AI assistants.
companies.lead_investor: Norwest Venture Partners, Greylock Partners
companies.score_rationale: Jerry Liu ex-Uber ML/Quora; strong applied-ML builder, popular OSS, not elite-lab

companies.name: Together AI
companies.description:
Together AI is an AI acceleration cloud platform founded in 2022, designed to help developers and AI researchers build, train, and deploy open-source generative AI models at scale. The company offers a decentralized infrastructure that reduces reliance on traditional cloud vendors, providing cost-effective and secure solutions for enterprise needs. With over 45,000 registered developers and rapid growth, Together AI is integrated into major AI development frameworks like LangChain, Vercel, and MongoDB.

The platform features a comprehensive suite of services, including AI inference-as-a-service, custom model training and fine-tuning, and access to a global GPU infrastructure. Users can execute open-source AI models with faster speeds and lower costs, while also having the option to develop proprietary models with full ownership. Together AI supports the AI research community by providing access to over 200 pre-trained models and contributing to various innovative projects. The company serves a diverse range of AI-native startups and has achieved notable success in enhancing performance and reducing costs for its clients.
companies.lead_investor: Lux Capital, Salesforce Ventures, SE Ventures, Long Journey Ventures, Nvidia, Definition Capital, March Capital Partners, Prosperity7vc, DMAC Capital Funding, Cadenza, Coatue Management, Kleiner Perkins, General Catalyst, Emergence, Brave Venture Capital, Greycroft Partners

companies.name: AssemblyAI
companies.description:
AssemblyAI is a San Francisco-based AI company founded in 2017 by Dylan Fox. The company specializes in advanced Speech AI models, offering developer-friendly APIs for speech recognition, transcription, and understanding in over 80 languages. AssemblyAI focuses on providing high-accuracy, real-time AI models that help businesses and developers create voice-powered applications efficiently.

The core offering includes a suite of Voice AI APIs that enable automatic speech-to-text transcription, speaker detection, topic detection, sentiment analysis, content moderation, and more. AssemblyAI's platform supports scalability for millions of daily calls and is designed to enhance the developer experience. The company has raised over $115 million in funding and has been recognized as one of Fast Company's 50 Most Innovative Companies of 2025. With a commitment to ongoing innovation, AssemblyAI continues to improve its models and features to meet the needs of its diverse customer base.
companies.lead_investor: Insight Partners, Smith Point Capital, Daniel Gross, Keith Block, Accel, Y Combinator, Nat Friedman

companies.name: Superlinked
companies.description:
Superlinked is a San Francisco-based AI startup founded in 2021. The company specializes in providing a Python framework and cloud infrastructure designed to assist AI engineers in building real-time search, recommendation, analytics, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) applications. Superlinked focuses on transforming complex structured and unstructured data into vector embeddings, enabling natural language queries over diverse data types.

The core offering includes an open-source Python framework and a cloud-based VectorOps platform that optimizes data retrieval in real time. Key features support enterprise use cases such as semantic search, recommendations, and analytics, while also allowing for local experimentation and seamless cloud deployment. Superlinked collaborates with data platforms like Starburst, Dataiku, and Redis to enhance its vector capabilities, targeting sectors such as e-commerce and enterprise AI. The company has raised significant funding and has secured its first customer contract, positioning itself as a valuable player in the information retrieval infrastructure space.
companies.lead_investor: Theory Ventures, 20SALES, Firestreak Ventures, Index Ventures
companies.score_rationale: CEO ex-Google, ex-YouTube; embeddings inference engine; mid-level not senior-elite

companies.name: Roboflow
companies.description:
Roboflow is a software development company that specializes in computer vision tools and platforms for developers and enterprises. Founded in 2019, the company launched its first platform in 2020 with the goal of simplifying computer vision development. Based in Des Moines, Iowa, Roboflow has raised $63.4 million in funding and serves over one million developers, including engineers at many Fortune 100 companies.

Roboflow offers a comprehensive end-to-end platform for building and deploying computer vision applications. This includes cloud-hosted image annotation tools, hosted model training solutions, API-based deployment options, and utilities for data preprocessing and annotation format conversion. The platform is designed to be extensible, allowing developers to integrate custom frameworks and technologies easily. Roboflow supports various industries, including healthcare, automotive, manufacturing, security, smart cities, and retail, providing solutions that enhance operational efficiency and decision-making.
companies.lead_investor: Amjad Masad, Craft Ventures, Guillermo Rauch, Y Combinator, GV, Frontline Ventures, Jeff Dean, Valor Ventures

companies.name: E2B
companies.description:
E2B is an open-source cloud infrastructure platform tailored for AI agents. It provides secure, sandboxed environments that allow for the execution of AI-generated code, long-running processes, and scalable agentic workflows. The platform supports various programming languages and integrates seamlessly with major AI models and frameworks.

Key offerings include secure sandboxes using Firecracker microVMs for total isolation, a Code Interpreter SDK for dynamic code execution, and full virtual computers that provide access to real-world tools. E2B also features enterprise capabilities such as bring-your-own-cloud options, on-premises deployment, and compliance with data regulations. The company supports startups with a program that offers $20,000 in usage credits for AI data analysis and app generation.

E2B is utilized by 88% of Fortune 100 companies and various AI startups, highlighting its effectiveness in AI-driven code execution and data exploration. The platform is designed with a developer-first approach, emphasizing security and scalability.
companies.lead_investor: Insight Partners, Decibel Partners, KAYA VC, Sunflower Capital, Scott Johnston
companies.score_rationale: Cloud for AI agents, Insight-backed, but founders no big-tech/research pedigree.

companies.name: PostHog
companies.description:
PostHog is an open-source platform designed for developers and engineering teams to analyze, test, observe, and deploy product features. Founded in 2020 and headquartered in San Francisco, the company has grown to approximately 106-150 employees. PostHog's mission is to empower engineers to make product decisions through a unified Product OS that handles real-time analytics, supporting billions of events with sub-second queries.

The platform offers over 10 paid products, including product analytics, session replay, A/B testing, and feature flags. It integrates seamlessly with data warehouses, providing a comprehensive toolkit for product-led growth and customer data management. PostHog serves over 100,000 customers and has a user base of 190,000 to 250,000 teams, focusing on engineering-led startups and scaling companies. The company emphasizes developer-first tools and aims to build every piece of SaaS needed for product success.
companies.lead_investor: Y Combinator, GV, Stripe, Peak XV Partners

companies.name: Prime Intellect
companies.description:
Prime Intellect is a decentralized AI infrastructure platform based in San Francisco, California. Founded in 2023, the company aims to democratize access to compute resources and facilitate the distributed training of large-scale AI models. It focuses on collective ownership of open AI innovations, making advanced AI development accessible to researchers, startups, and organizations.

The company offers a Compute Aggregation Platform that unifies access to global GPU resources, enabling users to utilize cost-effective and scalable computing power. Prime Intellect is also developing Distributed Training Frameworks for model development across diverse hardware infrastructures. Additionally, it provides a hub for open-source environments, access to verified training and evaluation stacks, and releases open-source AI models, including the INTELLECT-3 and INTELLECT-2. The platform supports decentralized reinforcement learning and offers a library of components for creating training environments. 

Prime Intellect targets researchers, academic institutions, AI startups, and organizations interested in training AI models, promoting permissionless participation in its initiatives.
companies.lead_investor: Brendan McCord, Founders Fund, Sandeep Nailwal, Balaji Srinivasan, Emad Mostaque, Jake Medwell, Andrej Karpathy, Menlo Ventures, Tri Dao, Dylan Patel
companies.score_rationale: Open superintelligence infra; founders credible in decentralized-AI but no clear elite big-tech/research past.

companies.name: Datacurve
companies.description:
Datacurve is an AI data company that operates a B2B marketplace and a gamified bounty platform called Shipd. Founded by Serena Ge and Charley Lee, the company focuses on generating high-quality coding datasets for training large language models (LLMs), particularly for complex software engineering tasks. Datacurve has raised $17.7 million in funding, including a $15 million Series A round led by Chemistry, with participation from notable investors.

The company transforms identified weaknesses in AI models into targeted data collection projects. It offers a range of services, including supervised fine-tuning data, reinforcement learning environments, and custom tasks for enterprise applications. Datacurve emphasizes high-volume production and quality control, attracting skilled engineers through a competitive bounty system. Its business model involves charging AI companies for custom datasets tailored to specific needs, positioning itself as a leader in specialized, expert-driven data solutions for advanced AI development.
companies.lead_investor: Mark Goldberg
companies.score_rationale: Frontier-model data founders, no big-tech/research pedigree on card.

companies.name: LangChain
companies.description:
LangChain is an AI infrastructure company founded in 2023 by Harrison Chase and Ankush Gola. It originated from an open-source Python framework launched in October 2022, aimed at simplifying the development of applications powered by large language models (LLMs). The company offers a range of tools for LLM orchestration, agent development, observability, and production deployment.

Key products include the open-source LangChain framework, which provides standardized interfaces for LLMs and integrates with various models and APIs. LangGraph is designed for flexible orchestration and building complex workflows, while LangSmith, launched in July 2023, offers observability and monitoring for LLM chains and agents. LangChain has gained significant traction, with a strong developer community reflected in its GitHub repository, which has over 111,000 stars. The company has also secured substantial funding, positioning itself for growth in the AI landscape.
companies.lead_investor: Databricks, IVP, Benchmark, Sequoia, Frontline, Datadog, Sapphire Ventures, CapitalG, Workday Ventures, ServiceNow Ventures

companies.name: Poolside
companies.description:
Poolside is an AI research and development company founded in 2023 by Jason Warner and Eiso Kant. The company specializes in creating generative AI foundation models tailored for software development and enterprise applications. Poolside's mission is to advance artificial general intelligence (AGI) by addressing complex software engineering challenges.

The company offers a full-stack generative AI coding platform that includes proprietary foundation models like Malibu, designed for deep reasoning and complex tasks, and Point, which provides real-time code suggestions. Additionally, Poolside features a generative AI assistant that interacts with developers, helping with code completion, reviews, and answering questions based on the context of their work. The platform is built to support enterprise-grade deployment, ensuring data privacy and compliance with governance standards.

Poolside targets enterprise software development organizations, aiming to automate routine tasks and enhance developer productivity. The company utilizes Amazon Web Services for its cloud infrastructure and has garnered investment from Adams Street Partners.
companies.lead_investor: Adams Street Partners, Nvidia, HSBC, Fin Capital, Bain Capital Ventures, LG Technology Ventures, StepStone Group, Premji Invest, Capital One Ventures, ISAI Gestion, Motier Ventures, BAM Elevate, eBay, Schroders Capital, Felicis, Redpoint, DST Global, Citi Ventures, The StepStone Group

companies.name: Firecrawl
companies.description:
Firecrawl, a developer-first platform founded in 2022, specializes in AI-ready web data infrastructure. Based in Delaware, the company provides a suite of APIs and tools designed for crawling, scraping, searching, and extracting structured data from the open web. Its core technology, the Fire Engine, is an AI-driven scraping engine that efficiently handles complex web data extraction challenges, delivering structured data faster and with higher success rates than traditional scrapers.

Firecrawl's offerings include a comprehensive Web Data API for AI, which supports various formats and interactive actions. The platform features intelligent search and extraction capabilities, real-time web indexing, and tools for lead enrichment. With a focus on reliability and performance, Firecrawl boasts impressive metrics, including over 350,000 users and significant growth in the past year. The company has attracted notable clients such as OpenAI, Alibaba.com, and PwC, serving a diverse range of developers and enterprises.
companies.lead_investor: Nexus Venture Partners
companies.score_rationale: YC S22 web-for-agents; founders built Mendable prior but no big-tech/research

companies.name: Yutori
companies.description: We are building AI agents that can reliably do everyday digital tasks for you on the web.
companies.score_rationale: Founders ex-Meta GenAI/FAIR Sr Directors + Georgia Tech professors — elite research+lab pedigree

companies.name: Baseten
companies.description:
Baseten is an AI infrastructure company based in San Francisco, founded in 2019. It provides a platform for deploying, serving, and fine-tuning machine learning models, focusing on high-performance inference for large language models and generative AI applications. The company enables engineering and ML teams to efficiently build and scale ML-powered applications, emphasizing performance, scalability, and cost-effectiveness.

The core platform offers end-to-end ML operations infrastructure, including model deployment and serving, inference optimization, and application building tools. Users can deploy custom or pre-trained models with minimal code, benefit from low-latency serving, and utilize a drag-and-drop interface for creating interactive user applications. Baseten operates on a pay-as-you-go pricing model, charging based on usage, which supports a wide range of applications across various industries, including fintech and healthcare.
companies.lead_investor: Battery Ventures, Greylock Partners, Blackbird Ventures, D.E. Shaw Group, Spark Capital, Wellington Management, IVP, Altimeter Capital, Sands Capital Management, Durable Capital Partners, Conviction, o1a, Verified Capital

companies.name: Town
companies.description: The unusually helpful AI assistant. Meet your Townie: https://www.town.com/sign-up
companies.score_rationale: Jean-Denis Greze: ex-CTO Plaid, ex-Dropbox eng — strong scaled-startup senior operator.

companies.name: Better Auth
companies.description:
Better Auth is a framework-agnostic authentication and authorization solution designed for TypeScript developers. It allows developers to create secure authentication systems directly on their backend and database, catering to a range of needs from simple sign-in flows to complex enterprise-grade systems. The platform includes features such as email and password authentication, social sign-on with multiple OAuth providers, two-factor authentication, multi-tenancy support, and enterprise-grade options like Single Sign-On.

Founded by Bereket Engida and Kinfe Michael Tariku, Better Auth is headquartered in San Francisco, California. The company operates as an open-source B2B solution and has received $5 million in seed funding from notable investors. Better Auth serves individual developers, startups, and established companies, and is recognized for its ease of use and comprehensive feature set. Its framework supports popular development tools like React, Vue, and Next.js, making it a versatile choice for modern web applications.
companies.lead_investor: P1 Ventures, Y Combinator, Peak XV Partners, Chapter One Ventures
companies.score_rationale: Creator of Better Auth (OSS); no big-tech-senior or research pedigree on card.

companies.name: Decagon
companies.description:
Decagon is Nigeria's leading tech training institute, established in 2018 by Chika Nwobi. Based in Lagos, it specializes in transforming talented individuals into skilled software engineers through structured training programs and career placement support. Since launching its first cohort in 2019, Decagon has successfully trained over 1,500 software engineers, achieving an impressive placement rate of over 80% for graduates.

The core offering is a six-month intensive software engineering program that includes comprehensive learning from basic programming to advanced practices. Participants engage in real-world assignments to build their portfolios and gain practical experience. Decagon also provides access to experienced tutors, industry mentors, and a supportive alumni network. In addition to its training programs, Decagon is expanding into international education placement services, helping learners pursue master's programs abroad. The institute is committed to equipping Nigeria's brightest talents with the skills and opportunities needed to thrive in the global tech ecosystem.
companies.lead_investor: Coatue

companies.name: Cline
companies.description:
Cline is an open-source AI coding agent tailored for developers, focusing on transparency and enterprise-grade features. It operates entirely on user infrastructure, ensuring that code remains under user control and providing clear visibility into spending. Cline has evolved from a project for hobbyists to a professional tool, recently securing $32 million in funding to enhance its enterprise capabilities.

Cline offers a range of AI-powered coding tools designed for various workflows. Key products include Cline for VS Code, which integrates AI capabilities into the popular code editor, and Cline CLI, a command-line interface for terminal-first developers. Additionally, Cline for JetBrains IDEs supports professional development environments like IntelliJ IDEA and PyCharm. The Cline Teams dashboard facilitates team management and budgeting, with plans for self-hosted options in the future. The platform emphasizes no-throttled context and open pricing based on true model costs.
companies.lead_investor: 1984 Ventures, Eric Simons, Jared Friedman, Essence Venture Capital, Emergence Capital, Addy Osmani, Cox Exponential, Theo Browne, Pace Capital, Logan Kilpatrick
companies.score_rationale: Saoud Rizwan solo founder, strong OSS traction (Cline) but no big-tech/research pedigree on card.

companies.name: Composio
companies.description:
Composio is an AI-native developer tools platform founded in 2023 by Soham Ganatra and Karan Vaidya in Bengaluru, India. The company specializes in infrastructure and APIs that allow AI agents and large language models (LLMs) to integrate with over 250 real-world applications, enhancing automation and AI capabilities. Composio supports more than 10,000 tools, including popular applications like GitHub, Notion, and Slack.

The platform offers a suite of services for rapid AI agent deployment, including extensive app integrations, managed authentication, real-time dashboards, and workflow automation tools. Composio emphasizes security with SOC 2 Type II compliance and provides support for various LLM providers. The company has attracted over 100,000 developers and secured $25 million in Series A funding, highlighting its strong developer experience and network effects. Pricing options range from a free Hobby tier to custom Enterprise solutions.
companies.lead_investor: agentfund.com, Lightspeed Venture Partners, Together, Blitzscaling Ventures, Guillermo Rauch, Soham Mazumdar, Gokul Rajaram, Dharmesh Shah, SV Angel, Elevation Capital
companies.score_rationale: Karan Vaidya: Rubrik + Google + IIT-Bombay + a16z Scout; strong-but-not-elite-senior.

companies.name: Adaption
companies.description:
Adaption is an AI research company dedicated to developing efficient intelligence that evolves in real-time. The company focuses on creating AI systems that prioritize efficiency in data and algorithms, allowing for dynamic adaptation. 

Adaption is actively expanding its team, seeking professionals such as Interface Research Engineers and Data Adaptation Research Engineers. This hiring reflects the company's commitment to advancing AI capabilities through a research-driven approach.
companies.lead_investor: Threshold Ventures, Fifty Years, Emergence, E14 Fund, Alpha Intelligence Capital, Neo vc, Mozilla Ventures
companies.score_rationale: Sara Hooker: PhD CS + serious AI research track record (ex-Cohere For AI/Google Brain pedigree).

companies.name: Braintrust
companies.description:
Braintrust is a decentralized talent network and AI recruiting platform that connects clients with technical freelancers. The platform offers services such as AI-powered matching and workforce management. It serves a variety of notable clients, including Nestle, Porsche, Atlassian, Goldman Sachs, Nike, NASA, and Google.

Additionally, there is another entity named Braintrust that operates as an AI observability platform. This platform focuses on evaluations, prompt engineering, and data management, featuring tools for batch testing, monitoring, and security compliance. Founded by Mike Knoop and others in San Francisco, this company emphasizes the importance of data integrity and performance in AI applications.
companies.lead_investor: Greylock Partners, ICONIQ Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, Elad Gil, Base Case Capital

companies.name: Retool
companies.description:
Retool is a low-code platform founded in 2017 that helps developers build custom internal tools and full-stack applications efficiently. The company, headquartered in San Francisco, focuses on reducing boilerplate coding and streamlining operations for businesses. With a team of around 300 employees, Retool has achieved significant growth, reaching over $75 million in annual recurring revenue within five years and becoming cash flow positive by late 2021.

Retool offers a drag-and-drop development environment featuring hundreds of UI components and backend integrations. Its core platform supports the creation of internal tools and workflows, while the Retool Portal allows for external-facing applications. The company also provides AI integrations for tasks like text summarization and image generation. Retool serves a diverse range of clients, from startups to Fortune 500 companies, enabling them to replace spreadsheets and one-off workflows with robust applications that support critical business functions.
companies.lead_investor: Patrick Collison, Daniel Gross, Sequoia, Caryn Marooney, John Collison, Nat Friedman, Elad Gil

companies.name: WorkOS
companies.description:
WorkOS is a modern API platform founded in 2019 that helps developers and SaaS startups integrate enterprise-ready features like authentication, identity management, and user access controls. The company is headquartered in San Francisco and operates as a remote-first team of about 95 people across the US and Canada. WorkOS has raised significant funding, including a $15M Series A and an $80M Series B.

The platform offers a suite of developer-first APIs, including Single Sign-On (SSO), Directory Sync (SCIM), and an Admin Portal for enterprise IT management. These tools simplify complex integrations, allowing SaaS companies to become "Enterprise Ready" and effectively serve large organizations. WorkOS emphasizes a strong developer experience with refined APIs and onboarding processes. The company is also carbon neutral and has gained traction with over 200 paying customers, including notable names like OpenAI, Vercel, and Webflow.
companies.lead_investor: Sapphire Ventures, Greenoaks Capital Partners, Meritech Capital, Abstract Ventures, Craft Ventures, Audacious Ventures

companies.name: AgentMail
companies.description:
AgentMail is an API-first email platform tailored for AI agents. It provides dedicated inboxes that allow these agents to send, receive, and manage emails while handling the necessary infrastructure. As a Y Combinator S25 company, AgentMail offers a solution that overcomes the limitations of traditional email providers, such as high subscription costs and restrictive rate limits.

The core product is an API platform that enables email agents to engage in conversations, automate workflows, and authenticate with third-party applications. Key features include programmatic inbox management, high-volume email handling without limits, real-time notifications, and tools for semantic search and data extraction. AgentMail is designed to enhance the developer experience and streamline email interactions for AI applications.
companies.lead_investor: General Catalyst, Y Combinator, Paul Graham, Dharmesh Shah, Taro Fukuyama, Karim Atiyeh, Paul Copplestone
companies.score_rationale: YC S25 agent infra; strong domain but no big-tech-senior/research pedigree on card.

companies.name: dbt Labs
companies.description:
dbt Labs, based in Philadelphia, was founded in 2016 as Fishtown Analytics and rebranded in 2021. The company focuses on its open-source data build tool (dbt) and related products that facilitate analytics engineering by transforming raw data into actionable insights within data warehouses. The dbt tool allows analysts and engineers to collaboratively build, test, and document SQL-based transformations, standardizing workflows in the modern data stack.

In addition to the core dbt tool, dbt Labs offers dbt Cloud, a commercial SaaS platform that provides hosted environments for scheduling, monitoring, and collaboration on data pipelines. The company has a strong community presence, with over 80,000 teams using its products weekly. dbt Labs is led by Tristan Handy, Drew Banin, and Connor McArthur, and operates offices in multiple cities, including San Francisco, New York, Austin, and Dublin.
companies.lead_investor: Fivetran

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.description:
OpenAI is an American artificial intelligence research organization based in San Francisco, California. Founded in December 2015, it aims to advance AI technology in ways that benefit humanity, focusing on the development of safe and beneficial artificial general intelligence (AGI). The organization was established by notable figures including Sam Altman and Elon Musk, with significant early investments contributing to a $1 billion endowment.

OpenAI has developed several innovative AI tools, including the OpenAI Gym for reinforcement learning, the GPT series of language models, and DALL-E for text-to-image generation. The organization transitioned to a hybrid "capped-profit" model in 2019, allowing it to raise capital while maintaining its mission. OpenAI collaborates closely with Microsoft, leveraging Azure for its technologies and ensuring ethical development in the AI field. Its core mission emphasizes promoting AGI that benefits humanity while ensuring safety and ethical considerations are prioritized.
companies.lead_investor: TPG, Andreessen Horowitz, D.E. Shaw Group, Amazon, Microsoft, Nvidia, SoftBank, T. Rowe Price, MGX

companies.name: Modal
companies.description:
Modal is a New York-based serverless compute platform that specializes in high-performance AI infrastructure. It enables developers and data teams to efficiently run compute-intensive workloads such as machine learning inference, training, and batch processing without the need to manage underlying infrastructure. Founded by Erik Bernhardsson and Akshat Bubna, Modal has expanded its presence with offices in New York, Stockholm, and San Francisco, achieving unicorn status with a valuation of $1.1 billion in September 2025.

The platform allows users to turn Python functions into autoscaling cloud workloads with GPU acceleration. Key features include a custom Rust-based container runtime, dynamic scaling across CPUs and GPUs, and real-time log streaming. Modal offers a comprehensive suite of services that covers the entire machine learning lifecycle, including model deployment, training, batch processing, and collaborative workspaces. It primarily serves AI/ML-focused technology companies, scientific research, and media sectors, with a pay-per-use pricing model that facilitates easy adoption for machine learning engineers and data teams.
companies.lead_investor: Lux Capital

companies.name: Uber
companies.description:
Uber is a global technology company founded in 2009, specializing in ride-hailing, food delivery, and logistics services. It started as UberCab in San Francisco, inspired by the challenges of hailing taxis in Paris. The company has grown significantly, operating in over 600 cities worldwide and offering a range of services that include UberX for standard rides, premium options for larger groups, and shared transport services.

In addition to ride-hailing, Uber has developed a robust food delivery service through Uber Eats and a logistics platform with Uber Rush for package delivery. The company emphasizes community engagement and job creation while adapting its services to meet local market needs. With a focus on convenience, the Uber app allows users to hail rides, track their drivers, and make payments in real-time.

companies.name: Meta
companies.description:
Meta Platforms, Inc., based in Menlo Park, California, is a leading technology company known for its social media and communication platforms. Originally founded in 2004 as TheFacebook, it rebranded to Facebook in 2005 and became Meta in 2021 to highlight its focus on the metaverse and future digital interactions. Meta operates several major platforms, including Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, Messenger, and Threads, which collectively serve 3.24 billion daily users.

The company generates significant revenue through advertising, accounting for 97.8% of its $189.46 billion annual income. Meta is also advancing in virtual and augmented reality technologies, with innovations like the Orion AR glasses and ongoing developments in AI tools. Its mission is to create technologies that foster human connection beyond traditional screens, helping businesses grow and connect with communities.

companies.name: Best Buy
companies.description:
Best Buy Co., Inc. is a leading American multinational consumer electronics retailer based in Richfield, Minnesota. Founded in 1966 as Sound of Music, the company has evolved from a specialty audio store to a major provider of electronics, appliances, and entertainment products across North America and select international markets. Best Buy operates a network of superstores and online platforms, offering a wide range of products including computing devices, mobile phones, home appliances, and entertainment systems.

The company is known for its customer-focused services, including Geek Squad, which provides 24/7 technical support and installation services. Best Buy emphasizes high-margin services to create recurring revenue and has pioneered omnichannel retailing. With thousands of stores and a diverse customer base, Best Buy continues to adapt and grow in the competitive electronics market.

companies.name: Linkedin
companies.description:
LinkedIn is a business-oriented social networking platform founded in December 2002 and launched in May 2003. Headquartered in Sunnyvale, California, it was acquired by Microsoft in 2016. The platform allows professionals to create profiles, connect with others, join industry groups, organize events, and share content such as articles and videos.

LinkedIn offers a variety of tools for professional networking, recruitment, marketing, and learning. Key features include profile creation, job postings, and LinkedIn Recruiter for HR professionals. The platform also provides targeted advertising solutions and premium subscriptions that offer enhanced privileges. Additionally, LinkedIn has an online learning division focused on skill development. Since its early days, LinkedIn has grown significantly, reaching millions of users and expanding internationally.

companies.name: Sondermind
companies.description:
SonderMind is a digital mental health platform that connects patients with licensed therapists and psychiatrists who accept their insurance. The company aims to make mental health services more accessible and user-friendly. It operates a national network of behavioral health providers and offers a technology platform that helps consumers find therapists that meet their needs and have available appointments.

SonderMind provides a range of mental health services, including therapy and psychiatry available in all 50 states, with options for virtual, in-person, or hybrid care. The platform features an AI-driven matching system to connect patients with suitable providers quickly. It also offers a mobile app for self-care resources and progress tracking. For practitioners, SonderMind provides administrative support, insurance credentialing, and an electronic health records suite. Additionally, the company offers enterprise solutions for employers, integrating mental health programs into workplace benefits.
companies.lead_investor: Premji Invest, Drive Capital

companies.name: Upside
companies.description:
Upside is a cash-back rewards platform that connects consumers with brick-and-mortar retailers, including gas stations, grocery stores, restaurants, and hardware stores. Users can earn cash back on everyday purchases, enhancing their shopping experience while helping businesses increase sales. The platform operates a marketplace that reaches over 35 million consumers through its app and partner networks, offering personalized promotions to drive transactions without disrupting retailer operations.

Founded around 2015 and headquartered in the US, Upside has offices in Austin, New York, and Chicago. The company is committed to sustainability, dedicating 1% of its revenue to initiatives like carbon offset projects and food rescue. Upside provides a free mobile app that allows users to earn cash back on various purchases and offers a retailer platform that delivers targeted promotions to boost foot traffic and profits. Additionally, it offers business tools, including personalized dashboards for tracking sales and profits.
companies.lead_investor: Builders VC, Bessemer Venture Partners, General Catalyst

companies.name: Block
companies.description:
Block, Inc., formerly known as Square, Inc., is a technology company based in San Francisco, founded in 2009 by CEO Jack Dorsey. The company focuses on economic empowerment through financial services, payments, and blockchain innovations. With around 17,000 employees and $23.5 billion in annual revenue, Block serves 57 million users and 4 million sellers, processing $241 billion in payments each year.

Block operates a range of interconnected brands that provide tools for payments, banking, lending, and bitcoin services. Its offerings include Square, a leading point-of-sale system; Cash App, a consumer digital wallet; Afterpay, a buy-now-pay-later service; and Bitkey, a self-custody bitcoin wallet. The company emphasizes integrated ecosystems to enhance access to the global economy and explores AI and blockchain technologies to improve financial efficiency and market reach.

companies.name: W&B from CoreWeave
companies.description:
Weights & Biases is an AI developer platform that offers a range of tools for building, tracking, managing, and deploying AI agents, applications, and machine learning models. Founded in 2017, the company aims to provide the best resources for AI developers. It serves thousands of companies and millions of practitioners globally, including teams at OpenAI, NVIDIA, and Cohere. In May 2025, Weights & Biases was acquired by CoreWeave, continuing its focus on tools for the generative AI era.

The platform features an integrated suite of products that support both LLMOps and MLOps workflows. Key offerings include W&B Weave for building AI applications, W&B Models for experiment tracking and visualization, and a Registry for sharing ML models and datasets. The platform is designed for enterprise use, ensuring compliance with various standards and providing robust security features. Weights & Biases is headquartered in San Francisco and has a dedicated team of 253 employees.
companies.lead_investor: CoreWeave

companies.name: G2i
companies.description:
G2i Inc. is a hiring platform based in Delray Beach, Florida, founded in 2012. The company specializes in connecting businesses with pre-vetted remote freelance and full-time engineers, particularly those skilled in technologies like React, GraphQL, and Node.js. G2i emphasizes developer health and employs video-based assessments for technical vetting, ensuring a rapid and efficient hiring process.

The platform features a pool of over 8,000 vetted professionals, including product managers and designers. G2i uses advanced matching techniques to align companies with engineers based on location, skills, and other factors. They offer a range of services, including compliance support, international payments, and customizable tech assessments. G2i also fosters a community of developers through mentorship and events, promoting a people-first culture with flexible work arrangements and a commitment to diversity hiring.

companies.name: Acrew Capital
companies.description:
Acrew Capital is a venture capital firm based in San Francisco, founded in 2019 by Lauren Kolodny and Theresia Gouw. The firm operates with a collaborative approach, consisting of five partners who work together to make investment decisions. Acrew Capital has raised $700 million in new capital, managing over $1.7 billion in total assets.

The firm employs a thesis-driven investment strategy, focusing on seed, early, and growth-stage investments across various sectors, including cybersecurity, fintech, healthcare, artificial intelligence, and e-commerce. Acrew typically invests between $1 million and $50 million, participating in rounds led by other investors as well. The firm is dedicated to supporting under-represented founders and has a geographic focus on the USA, Latin America, and Israel. Acrew Capital emphasizes long-term partnerships and aims to foster diversity in the tech industry through initiatives like the Diversify Capital Fund.

companies.name: Decawork
companies.description:
Decawork AI is a software development company that specializes in creating AI agents for IT support. With a small team, the company focuses on automating the resolution of IT support tickets, managing service requests, and handling access management. Their solutions are designed for IT service providers, Managed Service Providers (MSPs), and internal IT teams.

The company offers natural-language automation services that transform user instructions into actionable automations, enhancing productivity in IT operations. Decawork AI positions itself as an "AI for IT" solution provider, delivering tailored automation and AI services to meet the needs of modern IT environments. They also provide demo bookings for potential users, reflecting a commitment to customer engagement and adoption.

companies.name: Roku
companies.description:
Roku, Inc. is a leading American streaming technology company founded in 2002 by Anthony Wood. Headquartered in San Jose, California, Roku produces digital media players, smart TVs, and operates a robust advertising business on its platform. The company pioneered streaming to TVs with its first device in 2008 and has since become a market leader, reaching 145 million people across the U.S., Canada, Mexico, Australia, the UK, France, Germany, and Latin America.

Roku offers a range of products, including streaming players and Roku TVs, which come with an embedded operating system. Its platform aggregates various streaming services, providing users with easy access to content in one place. Roku also provides content distribution for publishers and advanced advertising tools, benefiting viewers, publishers, and advertisers alike. The company has established partnerships with TV manufacturers such as TCL and Hisense, enhancing its presence in the smart TV market.

companies.name: Zoox
companies.description:
Zoox is an American autonomous vehicle company and a subsidiary of Amazon, focused on designing and operating purpose-built robotaxis for urban mobility. Founded in 2014 and headquartered in Foster City, California, Zoox integrates vehicle design, autonomous driving technology, and ride-hailing services. The company aims to enhance urban transportation by offering safer and cleaner alternatives to traditional car ownership.

The flagship product of Zoox is a bidirectional, symmetrical robotaxi designed for autonomous ride-hailing. This vehicle accommodates up to four passengers and features advanced amenities for comfort and interaction. Zoox also provides an on-demand ride-hailing service through a mobile app, ensuring 24/7 availability without human drivers. The company launched its robotaxi service in Las Vegas in September 2025 and expanded to San Francisco in November 2025, offering free rides to select users in various neighborhoods. Zoox emphasizes electric vehicle technology and utilizes advanced technologies like AI and lidar sensors to support its mission.
companies.lead_investor: Amazon

companies.name: MongoDB
companies.description:
MongoDB is a NoSQL document-oriented database company founded in 2007 in New York City. The company was established by veterans of DoubleClick who aimed to address the limitations of traditional relational databases. Originally named 10gen, it rebranded to MongoDB, Inc. in 2013, reflecting its focus on its database product. The name "MongoDB" comes from "humongous," highlighting its capability to manage large volumes of data.

MongoDB's primary offerings include the MongoDB Database, a document-oriented NoSQL database designed for internet-scale applications, and MongoDB Atlas, a managed database-as-a-service platform available on major cloud providers. The company also provides enterprise features such as analytics, encryption, and migration tools, along with professional support and consulting services. Operating on an open-core model, MongoDB offers a free community server alongside paid support and enterprise features. The company has experienced significant growth, with a market capitalization reaching $10 billion in 2024.

companies.name: Atlassian
companies.description:
Atlassian Corporation is an Australian enterprise software company founded in 2002 by Mike Cannon-Brookes and Scott Farquhar. Based in Sydney, the company specializes in collaboration and productivity tools designed for software development, project management, and team workflows. With around 12,000 employees and offices in cities like Amsterdam and Bengaluru, Atlassian has grown significantly through product-led sales, focusing on self-service cloud and on-premises solutions.

The company is known for its core products, including Jira, a configurable issue and project tracker; Confluence, a documentation and knowledge-sharing tool; Bitbucket, a cloud code repository; and Jira Service Management, which is its fastest-growing product. Other offerings include Trello for visual project management and Opsgenie for incident management. Atlassian emphasizes values such as transparency and customer focus, and it supports teams with tools that facilitate continuous delivery and cloud-based collaboration.

companies.name: Stripe
companies.description:
Stripe, Inc. is a financial infrastructure platform founded in 2010 by brothers John and Patrick Collison. Headquartered in San Francisco, California, with additional offices in Dublin, London, Paris, Singapore, and Tokyo, Stripe specializes in online payment processing and related services for businesses globally. The company simplifies online transactions through developer-friendly APIs, making it easier for businesses to integrate payment solutions.

Stripe's core offerings include payment processing, fraud detection with Stripe Radar, business formation through Stripe Atlas, and merchant cash advances via Stripe Capital. The platform also automates sales tax calculations with Stripe Tax and provides no-code checkout options through Payment Links. Stripe has expanded its services to support various markets and has made significant strides in areas like Bitcoin support and carbon removal initiatives with Stripe Climate. The company has gained a strong reputation among developers and businesses of all sizes, contributing to its substantial growth and high valuation.

companies.name: Reddit
companies.description:
Reddit is a social media platform and online community where users can share and discuss content across thousands of user-created communities, known as subreddits. Founded in June 2005 by Steve Huffman and Alexis Ohanian, Reddit has grown to become one of the largest and most influential websites on the internet. The company is headquartered in San Francisco, California, and employs approximately 2,233 people.

Reddit operates as a social news aggregation and discussion forum, allowing registered users to submit content organized into various communities. The platform generates revenue through advertising products, sponsored content, and a subscription service called Reddit Gold. As of 2024, Reddit has around 2.2 million registered users and is recognized as a major source of online information, impacting a wide range of topics from news to niche interests. The company became publicly traded on March 21, 2024, under the ticker symbol RDDT.

companies.name: Vercel
companies.description:
Vercel is a cloud-based Frontend Cloud platform founded in 2015, originally known as Zeit until its rebranding in 2020. The company focuses on enhancing the front-end developer experience by providing tools for building, previewing, deploying, scaling, and securing high-performance web applications and websites. Vercel's core offering includes the open-source React framework Next.js, which supports both static and dynamic sites, and a cloud platform for deploying front-end applications.

The platform emphasizes ease of use with single-tap deployment, global speed, and accessibility. Vercel also offers AI-powered tools, such as v0, which generates unique user interfaces. The company serves a diverse range of clients, from individual developers to large enterprises like Walmart and Netflix, and has experienced significant growth, reaching a $200 million annual recurring revenue target by 2025. Vercel is backed by notable investors, including Accel and BlackRock, and aims to provide a comprehensive web development solution from idea to production.
companies.lead_investor: Accel, GV, IVP, Khosla Ventures, GIC, 8VC, Geodesic Capital, Bedrock

companies.name: Simular
companies.description:
Simular is an AI software company based in San Francisco, founded in 2024 by former DeepMind researchers Ang Li and Jiachen Yang. The company specializes in developing autonomous computer agents that perform digital tasks with high reliability across various platforms, including desktops, browsers, and mobile devices. Simular aims to reduce the time users spend on repetitive tasks, potentially saving up to five hours daily.

The company's core technology features a hybrid neuro-symbolic agent system that combines neural exploration and symbolic execution. Key products include Agent S, an open-agency framework, and Simular 1.0, a native desktop agent for macOS that automates complex workflows. Simular Cloud offers web-based access to these agents, allowing for real-time human control without local setup. The platform is designed for professionals in sectors like insurance, recruiting, and content creation, providing reliable automation solutions for repetitive digital work.
companies.lead_investor: South Park Commons, Felicis, NVentures

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.description:
Anthropic is an AI safety and research company founded in 2021 in San Francisco. It was established by Dario and Daniela Amodei, along with a team of former OpenAI researchers, to focus on developing reliable and interpretable AI systems that prioritize safety, ethics, and alignment with human values. As a public benefit corporation, Anthropic aims to balance profit with its mission to ensure that transformative AI benefits society.

The company’s core offerings include advanced AI models and research, particularly emphasizing safety through innovations like Constitutional AI. Its flagship product, Claude, is a conversational AI chatbot designed to be a safer alternative to existing models. Anthropic is also engaged in frontier AI research, exploring areas such as mechanistic interpretability and scalable oversight. The company collaborates with major tech firms like Google and Amazon, securing significant investments to support its mission and influence in the AI landscape.
companies.lead_investor: Blackstone, Insight Partners, Lightspeed Venture Partners, General Catalyst, ICONIQ Capital, Sequoia, D.E. Shaw Group, DST Global, Samsung Electronics, Greenoaks Capital Partners, Temasek, Fidelity Investments, Baillie Gifford, Dragoneer Investment Group, GIC, Coatue Management, Altimeter Capital, Micron Technology, SK Hynix, Brookfield Asset Management, Jane Street Capital, D1 Capital Partners, Capital Group, T. Rowe Price, NTT Venture Capital, XN, Situational Awareness, MGX, AMP

companies.name: Aviator
companies.description:
Aviator is a startup backed by Y Combinator and founded by former Google employees. The company provides an AI-powered development platform aimed at improving engineering workflows and boosting developer productivity. Aviator's mission is to make enterprise-grade engineering tools accessible to all developers, focusing on reducing friction in the development process.

The platform includes a range of AI-driven tools such as MergeQueue, which automates the merging of pull requests, and Automated Code Review, which identifies issues before they reach production. Other offerings include tools for code migrations, deployment management, and FlakyBot for managing flaky tests. Aviator emphasizes automating repetitive tasks in development, such as validating, testing, and monitoring, to enhance overall efficiency.
companies.lead_investor: Xoogler.co, Alumni Ventures

companies.name: Clickhouse
companies.description:
Langfuse is an open-source platform designed for large language model (LLM) engineering. Founded in 2022 during Y Combinator's W23 batch, the company is headquartered in Berlin, Germany, with an additional office in San Francisco. Langfuse provides tools for observability, metrics, evaluations, prompt management, and debugging, enabling teams to build and enhance production-grade LLM applications efficiently.

The platform supports any LLM model or framework and features hierarchical tracing, performance analytics, user feedback collection, and prompt management. It also offers interactive debugging and self-hosting options. Langfuse serves a diverse range of customers, including notable companies like Intuit, Twilio, and Khan Academy, focusing on engineering teams that require robust LLMOps solutions. The company is backed by prominent investors and has gained significant traction, with millions of SDK installs and Docker pulls.
companies.lead_investor: ClickHouse

companies.name: Amazon AGI Lab
companies.description:
Amazon is a leading e-commerce and cloud computing company based in Seattle, Washington. Founded by Jeff Bezos in 1994, it began as an online bookstore and has grown to become the world's largest internet-based retailer. The company focuses on simplifying online transactions and enhancing customer experiences.

Amazon offers a wide range of products and services, including online retail across various categories such as electronics, groceries, and toys. It provides third-party seller services and operates Amazon Web Services (AWS), a major cloud computing platform. The company also features digital products like the Kindle e-reader, Alexa smart speakers, and Prime Video streaming service. Additionally, Amazon Prime offers subscription services for fast delivery and exclusive content.

With a customer base of hundreds of millions across more than 20 countries, Amazon is dedicated to improving customer satisfaction through competitive pricing, extensive product selection, and efficient delivery options. The company continues to expand its offerings and innovate in the retail and technology sectors.

companies.name: Anterior
companies.description:
Anterior is an AI company based in New York City, founded in 2022 by Zahid Mahmood and Abdel Mahmoud. The company specializes in an AI-powered clinical co-pilot platform designed for health plans. This platform automates labor-intensive workflows that require clinical reasoning, such as prior authorizations and utilization management. Anterior aims to streamline healthcare administration, making processes as seamless as tapping a credit card.

Headquartered at 175 Varick St, Anterior employs around 46 people and has raised $23.2 million in funding, including a Seed round led by Sequoia Capital and a $20 million Series A round led by New Enterprise Associates. The company’s platform includes features for utilization management, case management, risk adjustment, payment integrity, disease management, quality improvement, member services, and population health. Its AI agent, Florence, enhances prior authorization processes and supports various administrative functions.
companies.lead_investor: Kinnevik, Sequoia, NEA, FPV Ventures

companies.name: Hugging Face
companies.description:
Hugging Face, Inc. is a New York City-based company founded in 2016 by Clément Delangue, Julien Chaumond, and Thomas Wolf. It specializes in developing computational tools and a platform for machine learning applications, particularly in natural language processing (NLP). Initially launched as a chatbot app, the company transitioned to an open-source hub for sharing machine learning models, datasets, and applications.

The core offerings of Hugging Face include the Transformers Library, a popular open-source library for NLP that supports models like BERT and GPT. They also provide a Model and Dataset Hub for collaboration and discovery of machine learning resources. Their enterprise products, introduced around 2020-2021, feature AutoTrain for automated model training and an Inference API for model deployment. Hugging Face has built a strong community and serves over 10,000 organizations, including notable clients like Intel, Qualcomm, and Pfizer.
companies.lead_investor: Amazon, Sound Ventures, Nvidia, Alphabet | Google, IBM, Salesforce, Qualcomm, AMD, Intel Corporation

companies.name: DatologyAI
companies.description:
DatologyAI is an artificial intelligence company based in Redwood City, California, founded in 2023 by former researchers and engineers from Google’s DeepMind, Twitter, and MosaicML. The company specializes in automated data curation tools designed for training deep learning models. With a team of 24 employees, DatologyAI operates as a privately held entity.

The platform offers automated data selection, which identifies and prioritizes valuable data points while filtering out unnecessary information. Its modality-agnostic algorithms work across various data types, making the technology applicable in multiple industries. DatologyAI's solutions are designed to handle large datasets efficiently, enabling organizations to train models faster, better, and with smaller resource requirements. The company aims to help clients achieve significant improvements in model performance and cost-effectiveness. Ari Morcos is the CEO of DatologyAI.
companies.lead_investor: Viviana Faga, Astasia Myers, Amplify Partners, Radical Ventures, Amazon Alexa Fund, Elad Gil, M12

companies.name: Superconductor
companies.description: The best platform to build software with AI agents in the cloud.

companies.name: Ramp
companies.description:
Ramp is a financial technology company based in New York City, with additional offices in Miami and San Francisco. It offers an all-in-one finance operations platform designed to streamline corporate charge cards, expense management, bill payments, procurement, and accounting automation. This platform helps businesses of all sizes save time and money by integrating multiple finance functions into a single, intuitive system.

Founded as Ramp Business Corporation, Ramp serves a diverse clientele, including startups, mid-market companies, and global enterprises. The company has achieved significant growth, reaching a $32 billion valuation and reporting $1 billion in annualized revenue. With over 50,000 customers, Ramp emphasizes customer success, enabling users to save more than $600 million and 27.5 million hours through its services. The platform supports payments in 195 countries and offers features like AI-powered expense tracking, automated bill payments, and real-time insights, making it a comprehensive solution for financial efficiency.
companies.lead_investor: Insight Partners, Lightspeed Venture Partners, Stripes, General Catalyst, ICONIQ Capital, Thrive Capital, D.E. Shaw Group, Goldman Sachs, Founders Fund, BoxGroup, Khosla Ventures, Alpha Wave Global, GIC, Coatue Management, Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan, 137 Ventures, 8VC, Generation Investment Management, Avenir Growth Capital, D1 Capital Partners, T. Rowe Price, BroadLight Capital, Definition Capital, 1789 Capital, Morgan Stanley Investment Management, Pinegrove Venture Partners, Sands Capital Ventures

companies.name: Perceptron AI
companies.description:
Perceptron AI is a research company founded in 2024 by Armen Aghajanyan and Akshat Shrivastava. Based in Carnation, Washington, the company focuses on developing foundation models that connect digital AI with real-world applications. Their mission is to create a layer of intelligence that bridges the physical and digital worlds, aiming to build Physical AGI (Artificial General Intelligence).

The company has received early-stage funding from notable investors, including Bessemer Venture Partners, S32, and Foundation Capital, as well as support from The Explorer Fund. Perceptron AI employs a team of 16 people and is classified as a Research Services company. Their innovative approach treats various data streams—such as video, audio, text, and structured data—equally, allowing for real-time performance across different hardware configurations. This multimodal foundation model architecture enables the system to process and understand information from multiple sensory inputs, enhancing interactions with the physical world.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.description:
Microsoft Corporation is a leading American multinational technology company based in Redmond, Washington. Founded in 1975 by Bill Gates and Paul Allen, Microsoft initially focused on developing programming languages. The company gained prominence by creating the MS-DOS operating system for IBM's first personal computer, which established its market dominance.

Microsoft's product offerings include operating systems like Windows, productivity software such as Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, and cloud computing services through Azure. The company also has a presence in the gaming industry with its Xbox console. Recognized as the largest software company by revenue, Microsoft has consistently maintained a strong market position and is one of the most valuable brands globally. The company has evolved under the leadership of notable CEOs, including Bill Gates, Steve Ballmer, and currently, Satya Nadella.

companies.name: AI21
companies.description:
AI21 Labs is an Israeli AI company founded in 2017 by notable figures in the field, including Professor Amnon Shashua, Professor Yoav Shoham, and Ori Goshen. The company focuses on developing trustworthy enterprise AI systems and foundation models that enhance productivity through reliable and secure solutions. AI21 Labs specializes in generative AI, providing customizable AI tools and models that integrate seamlessly into existing systems.

The company offers a range of high-performance models, including the Jurassic and Jamba series, designed for various enterprise applications. Their AI21 Maestro system enables the rapid creation and deployment of agents that automate data-intensive tasks. Additionally, Wordtune serves as a writing assistant to enhance human-machine collaboration. AI21 Labs targets industries such as tech, gaming, and education, partnering with companies like Latitude and Ubisoft to deliver innovative solutions that prioritize efficiency and compliance.
companies.lead_investor: Intel Capital, Pitango Venture Capital, Nvidia, Comcast Ventures, Samsung Next, b2venture, Ahren Innovation Capital, SCB10X, Walden Catalyst Ventures, Alphabet | Google

companies.name: Red Hat
companies.description:
Red Hat, Inc. is a leading American software company that specializes in open-source solutions for enterprises. Founded in 1993 and headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, Red Hat has grown to operate in over 40 countries. The company became a part of IBM in 2019, following a significant acquisition valued at approximately $34 billion. Red Hat is recognized for its commitment to open-source principles and has achieved notable revenue milestones, reaching nearly $3 billion by 2018.
