The company's primary offerings include Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL), a stable operating system designed for datacenters and cloud environments, and Fedora, a community-driven version that fosters innovation. Red Hat also provides a range of services, including technical support and system management through the Red Hat Network. The company has established strong partnerships with major technology vendors like Dell, IBM, HP, and Oracle, enhancing its capabilities in cloud computing, storage, and virtualization.

companies.name: Google
companies.description:
Google is a multinational technology company founded in 1998 by Larry Page and Sergey Brin in California. Initially developed as a search engine at Stanford University, Google has grown into a global leader in technology, focusing on facilitating access to information for users worldwide. The company is headquartered in Mountain View, California, known as the Googleplex.

Google offers a wide range of products and services. Its core offering is the search engine, which organizes and ranks information to provide relevant results. Other key services include Gmail for email, Google Workspace for productivity, and YouTube for video sharing. Google also leads in advanced technologies, including AI and quantum computing. The company generates revenue primarily through its advertising platforms, which include tools for transparency in ad targeting and election ads. With billions of users accessing its services daily, Google continues to innovate and expand its offerings across various sectors.
companies.lead_investor: Astound

companies.name: Allos AI
companies.description: Making innovative generics accessible with Causal AI
companies.lead_investor: Berkeley SkyDeck Accelerator, Oxford Science Enterprises, Habico Invest

companies.name: Amplify Partners
companies.description:
Amplify Partners is a venture capital firm founded in 2012, focusing on early-stage investments in technology companies. The firm specializes in supporting technical founders who are addressing complex infrastructure challenges. Amplify invests in sectors such as developer infrastructure, data tooling, AI, security, and digitally native biology, emphasizing products where AI or distributed-systems expertise is a core advantage.

Based in the Bay Area/Menlo Park, California, Amplify typically leads Seed and Series A rounds, with investments ranging from $100K to $10M. The firm reserves a significant portion of its funds for follow-on investments to assist founders in achieving product-market fit. Amplify also offers hands-on operational support through its internal Build Team, helping portfolio companies with growth strategies, hiring, and go-to-market efforts. Additionally, Amplify fosters a community of over 150 technical founders, providing networking opportunities and resources through various events and workshops.

companies.name: Warp
companies.description:
Warp is an AI-native development environment that helps developers build, deploy, and maintain software with the assistance of multi-agent AI directly integrated into the terminal. Founded in 2020 and based in New York, Warp has raised $50 million in funding and serves over half a million engineers.

The company offers a Rust-based, GPU-accelerated terminal designed to enhance the command-line interface. Key features include multi-model AI agents that access leading models from OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google, full software lifecycle integration, and interactive terminal capabilities. Developers can generate and edit code with real-time visibility and control, create detailed roadmaps, and monitor production effectively. Warp targets modern developers and tech companies looking for advanced programming solutions and positions itself as a leader in AI-driven development tools. The company's mission is to make the command line more intuitive and collaborative, automating the software development lifecycle from start to finish.
companies.lead_investor: Sequoia, Marc Benioff, Jeff Weiner, BoxGroup, Neo vc, Dylan Field, Sam Altman, GV

companies.name: Box
companies.description:
Box, Inc. is a leading independent cloud content management platform that focuses on secure storage, sharing, collaboration, and workflow automation for enterprise content. Founded in 2005, Box transitioned from a consumer-focused service to an enterprise solution, emphasizing security and compliance. The company is headquartered in Redwood City, California, and has seen significant growth since its inception.

Box offers a SaaS platform that manages the full content lifecycle. Its features include cloud storage and file sharing, collaboration tools for real-time editing, and advanced security measures tailored for enterprises. The platform also supports integrations and automation through APIs, enhancing its functionality with features like AI and e-signatures. Box serves a variety of industries, including financial services, healthcare, media, technology, and government, catering to businesses of all sizes.

companies.name: Zo Computer
companies.description:
Zo Computer is an AI-powered personal cloud computer company based in Brooklyn, New York. Founded in 2013 by Ben and Rob, the company focuses on democratizing expert-level computing. It offers fully customizable digital workspaces that combine AI capabilities with server functionality. Users can host applications, run automations, and generate custom software on demand.

Key features of Zo include access to advanced AI language models for coding and task execution, file storage, web hosting, and app connectivity. The platform allows for instant scalability and provides a language-driven interface that simplifies complex tasks. Zo emphasizes user-owned infrastructure, promoting a decentralized internet experience where individuals can create and manage their own digital environments. The company's mission is to redefine computers as flexible tools powered by AI, enhancing privacy and personal interactions online.

companies.name: Google DeepMind
companies.description:
Google DeepMind is an artificial intelligence research company founded in London in 2010. Acquired by Google in 2014, it became a subsidiary of Alphabet Inc. in 2025, merging Google Brain and DeepMind into a unified team led by CEO Demis Hassabis. The company is headquartered in London and has research centers in several countries, including Canada, France, Germany, Switzerland, and the United States.

The mission of Google DeepMind is to develop safe and beneficial AI systems that address complex real-world challenges. The organization employs deep learning and reinforcement learning techniques, utilizing advanced neural networks to analyze and interpret data. Notable technologies developed by Google DeepMind include AlphaGo, AlphaFold, and WaveNet, among others. Their work spans various sectors, including healthcare, energy efficiency, software development, and advertising, contributing to advancements in these fields. Google DeepMind has also been recognized for its influence in the AI industry, being named one of TIME's 100 Most Influential Companies in 2025.
companies.lead_investor: Alphabet | Google

companies.name: Intercom
companies.description:
Intercom is a customer messaging platform founded in August 2011 by Eoghan McCabe, Des Traynor, David Barrett, and Ciaran Lee. The company aims to enhance personalized communication between businesses and their customers. It originated from the founders' experiences with an error-tracking app, leading them to create the first in-app messenger to facilitate direct interactions.

Intercom offers a suite of customer support and messaging tools that enhance communication throughout the customer journey. Key features include the Business Messenger for real-time conversations, automated and targeted messaging for personalized outreach, and conversational support tools that engage and assist customers effectively. The platform is designed to help businesses build strong relationships with their customers, integrating seamlessly into their existing systems. Intercom has experienced significant growth, serving thousands of companies and expanding its offerings to meet the evolving needs of customer communication.
companies.lead_investor: Salesforce

companies.name: EliseAi
companies.description:
EliseAI is an agentic AI platform that automates complex operational workflows in the housing and healthcare sectors. Founded in 2017 and based in New York, the company has around 400 employees across four offices. EliseAI's technology goes beyond traditional chatbots by utilizing AI agents that manage full end-to-end workflows, enabling 24/7 responses and task completion through various channels like voice, SMS, email, and web chat.

In the housing sector, EliseAI automates leasing communication, tour scheduling, maintenance requests, and billing processes. It also offers AI-Guided Tours, a self-guided touring technology for property management. In healthcare, the platform streamlines intake forms, scheduling, and patient communication. EliseAI serves multifamily operators, property owners, property managers, and healthcare organizations, delivering measurable results such as improved customer experiences and increased efficiency. The company has raised $35 million in funding and generates $31.5 million in revenue.
companies.lead_investor: Bessemer Venture Partners, Andreessen Horowitz, Sapphire Ventures, Navitas Capital

companies.name: Unblocked
companies.description:
Unblocked is a contextual code intelligence platform designed to assist software development teams in understanding their codebases. The platform offers AI-powered insights about code, documentation, and team knowledge, enabling teams to write better code, conduct thorough reviews, and onboard new developers efficiently.

Founded by Dennis Pilarinos, Unblocked is based in Vancouver and has raised $30 million in funding, including a $20 million Series A round in May 2025. The platform integrates with popular development tools like Slack, Jira, and Google Drive, enhancing AI coding assistants with contextual understanding. Key features include contextual code intelligence, AI-powered code review, developer onboarding support, code search and navigation, and autonomous CI triage. Unblocked also prioritizes security with enterprise-grade features such as SOC 2 Type II certification and role-based access control.

The company serves clients like Drata, AppDirect, Big Cartel, and TravelPerk, helping engineers save time and improve productivity. Unblocked is backed by notable venture capital firms and angel investors, including Stewart Butterfield, founder of Slack.
companies.lead_investor: First Round Capital, Amplify Partners, Radical Ventures, XYZ Capital, B Capital Group

companies.name: Morgan Stanley
companies.description:
Bucknell University is a private liberal arts university located in Lewisburg, Pennsylvania. Founded in 1846, it has a rich history and is recognized as the nation's 100th college or university. The university has an endowment of $1.17 billion and serves over 3,800 undergraduate students along with about 50 graduate students on its expansive 450-acre campus.

Bucknell offers a wide range of academic programs, providing bachelor's and master's degrees in 64 majors across various fields, including arts and sciences, business, engineering, and education. The university is structured into three colleges: the College of Arts & Sciences, the College of Engineering, and the Freeman College of Management. Students benefit from diverse opportunities, including study abroad programs and access to research facilities like an observatory and environmental preserve. 

Campus life at Bucknell is vibrant, featuring 27 NCAA Division I varsity sports teams and a strong sense of community among students, faculty, and alumni. The university encourages interdisciplinary exploration, allowing students to pursue their passions across different fields.

companies.name: Paper Compute Company
companies.description: Distributed systems primitives for AI agents. Telemetry, orchestration, and operator tooling that keeps your workloads durable, observable, and self healing.

companies.name: Millennium
companies.description:
Millennium Management is a global, diversified alternative investment firm founded in 1989, managing over $83.5 billion in assets. Headquartered in New York City, the firm operates in more than 140 locations worldwide and employs around 6,500 professionals across over 330 investment teams.

The firm's mission is to deliver high-quality returns for investors through a combination of scale, specialization, and an entrepreneurial culture. Millennium utilizes an entrepreneurial investing model, empowering skilled professionals with the resources and technology needed to pursue a diverse range of investment strategies, including fundamental equity and equity arbitrage. With a focus on capital stability and a rigorous risk framework, Millennium aims to provide consistent long-term returns while actively managing a portfolio valued at approximately $207 billion.

companies.name: FriendliAI
companies.description:
FriendliAI is a generative AI infrastructure company founded in 2021, based in Redwood City, California, with roots in Seoul, South Korea. The company specializes in high-performance platforms for deploying and optimizing large language models (LLMs) and custom AI agents. FriendliAI aims to make AI accessible for businesses of all sizes by addressing challenges such as high costs, latency, and scalability.

The core offering, the Friendli Engine, provides ultra-low latency inference and supports custom generative AI models. It features advanced monitoring, autoscaling, and real-time data integration for various applications, including chatbots and customer service. FriendliAI also offers dedicated endpoints for managed cloud services and serverless deployment options for fine-tuned models. The platform integrates with hubs like Hugging Face and Weights & Biases, ensuring seamless model import and deployment. With a focus on reliability and performance, FriendliAI serves a diverse range of industries, including automotive, education, finance, healthcare, and media.
companies.lead_investor: Capstone Partners, Sierra Ventures, KB Securities, Alumni Ventures, Korea Development Bank

companies.name: Airbyte
companies.description:
Airbyte is an open-source data integration platform that specializes in Extract, Load, Transform (ELT) processes. It enables users to sync data from over 600 sources, including APIs, databases, and SaaS applications, to various destinations like data warehouses and lakes. The platform supports both cloud-hosted and self-managed deployments, catering to the needs of over 7,000 companies with more than 170,000 deployments.

The platform features a modular, cloud-native architecture with a user-friendly web app interface. It offers a large library of pre-built connectors, a Connector Development Kit for custom connectors, and tools for automating data pipelines. Airbyte's Enterprise Edition includes advanced features such as compliance with GDPR and HIPAA, high availability, and 24/7 support. The company serves a diverse range of industries, including financial services, healthcare, and automotive, focusing on data sovereignty and security.
companies.lead_investor: Salesforce Ventures, Benchmark, Coatue Management, Altimeter Capital, Thrive Capital, Accel, SV Angel

companies.name: Adobe
companies.description:
Adobe Inc. is a prominent American software company founded in December 1982 by John Warnock and Charles Geschke. Headquartered in San Jose, California, Adobe specializes in creative software, digital document standards, and digital marketing solutions. The company is known for its innovative products that have shaped the fields of graphic design, video editing, and digital publishing.

Adobe's key offerings include industry-standard tools such as Photoshop for image editing, Illustrator for vector graphics, InDesign for page layout, and Premiere Pro for video editing. The company also provides Acrobat for PDF creation and editing, along with a suite of digital marketing solutions through its Experience Cloud. Adobe Creative Cloud bundles many of these tools, making them accessible to creative professionals, publishers, and enterprises.

With a strong focus on collaboration and integration, Adobe partners with major companies like Apple, Microsoft, and IBM, ensuring its tools are widely compatible across various platforms. The company's products are essential for professionals in advertising, print, and digital media.

companies.name: Abridge
companies.description:
Abridge is an AI-powered clinical documentation platform that converts patient-clinician conversations into structured clinical notes for electronic health records (EHRs). Founded in 2018, the company focuses on enhancing healthcare communication through its enterprise-grade ambient AI platform, which automatically documents patient encounters during in-person or telehealth visits. Abridge's technology generates real-time, billable notes aligned with the SOAP format and integrates seamlessly with Epic EHR systems.

The platform features advanced capabilities, including a Contextual Reasoning Engine that creates compliant notes by utilizing data from previous encounters and clinician preferences. Abridge also offers patient visit summaries that improve patient comprehension and has developed nursing documentation solutions in collaboration with Mayo Clinic and Epic. The company serves a wide range of healthcare providers, including major systems like Kaiser Permanente and Johns Hopkins Medicine, and is recognized for its commitment to security and compliance with certifications such as ISO 27001 and HIPAA.
companies.lead_investor: Eli Lilly

companies.name: General Reasoning Inc.
companies.description:
General Reasoning is an American AI research and infrastructure startup founded in 2024 and based in San Francisco. The company specializes in reinforcement-learning (RL) environments and reasoning-data infrastructure, focusing on the post-training stage of frontier foundation-model development. Its mission is to provide critical RL-environment infrastructure that supports the training of foundation models through structured environments, verifiable rewards, and evaluation layers.

The company offers a flagship RL-environment infrastructure platform, along with tooling for reasoning-data, simulation, and reward-signal integration. General Reasoning targets frontier-AI labs and enterprise foundation-model trainers that require external RL infrastructure. With a team of 11-50 employees, the company has secured $10.9 million in funding to further its goal of advancing toward universal intelligence.

companies.name: YouTube
companies.description:
YouTube is an American online video-sharing and social media platform, founded in 2005 by Chad Hurley, Steve Chen, and Jawed Karim. Headquartered in San Bruno, California, it was acquired by Google in 2006. YouTube allows users to upload, share, view, and discover a wide range of videos, supporting user-generated content across various categories such as entertainment, education, and music.

Key features of YouTube include video uploading and streaming, YouTube Analytics for creators to track viewer engagement, and YouTube Live for real-time streaming. The platform also offers subscription services, including ad-free music videos. YouTube has evolved to support content creators with monetization tools and has integrated with Google's infrastructure to enhance its services.
companies.lead_investor: Mission Essential Personnel

companies.name: Temporal
companies.description:
Temporal Technologies is an open-source platform that specializes in stateful microservices orchestration. Founded in 2019 by former AWS engineers Samar Abbas and Maxim Fateev, the company is based in Bellevue, Washington. Temporal enables developers to create reliable applications by managing complex, long-running workflows with features like automatic retries, state persistence, and fault tolerance.

The platform offers a durable execution environment for workflows-as-code, replacing traditional state machines and custom retry logic. Key capabilities include workflow orchestration for coordinating asynchronous tasks, which ensures reliability during failures. Temporal supports both self-hosted open-source options and fully managed cloud services, catering to a wide range of use cases such as e-commerce order processing and CI/CD pipelines. The company serves over 1,500 paying customers, including notable names like Snapchat, Netflix, and Stripe, and has raised significant funding to support its growth.
companies.lead_investor: Index Ventures, Lightspeed Venture Partners, Tiger Global, Andreessen Horowitz, Sequoia, Sapphire Ventures, Amplify Partners, GIC, Madrona Venture Group

companies.name: Sourcegraph
companies.description:
Sourcegraph is a code intelligence platform founded in 2013 by Quinn Slack and Beyang Liu. Headquartered in San Francisco, California, the company operates as an all-remote organization. Sourcegraph has raised $223 million in funding from prominent venture capital firms, including Andreessen Horowitz and Sequoia Capital. The platform aims to enhance developers' ability to understand, fix, and automate code across large and complex codebases.

The core offerings of Sourcegraph include Universal Code Search, which allows users to locate code snippets and trace dependencies, and Code Navigation and Exploration, which improves productivity and onboarding. Additionally, the platform features Batch Changes for automating large-scale code modifications, Code Insights for visibility into codebases, and Cody, an AI-powered coding assistant that aids in understanding code and automating tasks. Sourcegraph provides both self-hosted and cloud deployment options, emphasizing security and scalability. The company serves a diverse range of clients, including major organizations like Uber and Plaid.
companies.lead_investor: Insight Partners, Geodesic Capital, Andreessen Horowitz

companies.name: Lease End
companies.description:
The end-of-lease process is intentionally confusing. Automakers and dealerships benefit when consumers feel rushed, under-informed, or unsure of their options. When that happens, drivers often hand back vehicles with real equity left on the table — equity that quietly turns into profit for someone else.

Lease End exists to change that.

We give drivers clarity, leverage, and control at the exact moment they'd typically be left in the dark.

As a technology-driven fintech platform, Lease End helps consumers understand the true value of their leased vehicle and the full range of options available to them, whether that's buying it out through financing or walking away with confidence. Our free tools are built to surface the numbers that matter, and our financial specialists are trained to advocate for the driver, not the dealership.

No pressure. No hidden incentives. No fine print games.

Our mission: To provide the most loved and loving end-of-lease experience—every time.

Because the end of a lease shouldn't be the moment you lose control. It should be the moment you finally get it back. 🚗💨

companies.name: Maven Clinic
companies.description:
Maven Clinic is a virtual health platform founded in 2014 by CEO Kate Ryder, focusing on continuous care for women and families. It specializes in areas such as fertility, family-building, pregnancy, parenting, pediatrics, mental health, and menopause. As the largest women's and family telehealth network, Maven offers services through a web and app-based platform, connecting members to over 2,000 providers across more than 30 specialties and 350 subspecialties in 175 countries.

Maven's key offerings include fertility and family-building support, maternity and postpartum benefits, pediatric and menopause care, and integrated mental health services. The platform also features the Maven Wallet for benefits administration and the Maven Manage Benefit for fertility management. With 15 million lives under management, Maven partners with over 2,000 employers and health plans, including many Fortune 15 companies, to provide personalized benefits. The company has received significant recognition, including being named one of TIME's 100 Most Influential Companies in 2023.
companies.lead_investor: Lux Capital, Oak HC/FT, Icon Ventures, StepStone Group, Sequoia, General Catalyst, Dragoneer Investment Group

companies.name: Docker
companies.description:
Docker, Inc. is a technology company that provides developer tools for building, sharing, and running containerized applications. Originating from an open-source project, Docker simplifies app development by allowing developers to package applications with their dependencies into lightweight, portable containers. This ensures consistency across different environments, from development to production.

Founded in 2008 as DotCloud, Docker rebranded in 2013 and has since gained significant traction, serving 10 million registered developers and supporting 35 million Docker Engine downloads monthly. The company focuses on developer-centric solutions, including Docker Engine, Docker Desktop, and Docker Hub, which facilitate the creation, testing, and distribution of container images. Docker emphasizes sustainable growth and open-source advocacy, addressing modern challenges in software development and security.
companies.lead_investor: Insight Partners, Benchmark, Bain Capital Ventures, Vertex Ventures US, Four Rivers Group, Atlassian Ventures, Tribe Capital

companies.name: Unsloth
companies.description:
Unsloth AI is a San Francisco-based startup founded in 2023 by brothers Daniel and Michael Han. The company specializes in open-source tools designed to accelerate the fine-tuning and reinforcement learning of large language models (LLMs). With a team of 8 employees, Unsloth AI is part of Y Combinator's Summer 2024 batch and aims to make LLM training significantly faster and more efficient, achieving up to 30 times speed improvements and 90% less memory usage compared to existing methods.

The company offers a core fine-tuning engine that enhances LLM training on both single and multi-GPU setups. It supports various models and provides tools for reinforcement learning customization. Unsloth AI also offers free open-source versions for individual developers and enterprises, along with additional features like Docker support and model downloads via Hugging Face. The founders bring valuable experience, with Daniel Han previously working at NVIDIA, optimizing algorithms for improved performance.

companies.name: Cerebras
companies.description:
Cerebras Systems Inc. is an American artificial intelligence company founded in 2015, based in Sunnyvale, California, with additional offices in San Diego, Toronto, and Bangalore. The company specializes in wafer-scale computing systems, offering some of the fastest AI inference and training platforms for complex deep learning applications. Cerebras has raised over $720 million in funding, with significant milestones including the launch of the CS-2 system featuring the Wafer Scale Engine 2 and the upcoming CS-3 system powered by the WSE-3.

Cerebras provides on-premise supercomputers like the CS-2 and CS-3, designed for large AI workloads in training and inference. These systems enable rapid AI applications and complex reasoning tasks. The company also offers cloud services through private APIs, supporting dedicated capacity for various models and data. Collaborations, such as with Dell Technologies, enhance their AI compute infrastructure, making it suitable for diverse applications in fields like medical research, cryptography, and energy.

companies.name: Bugcrowd
companies.description:
Bugcrowd is a cybersecurity company based in San Francisco, founded in 2012. It offers a crowdsourced platform for penetration testing, vulnerability disclosure, and security risk management. The company helps organizations identify and address vulnerabilities across various applications, including web, API, mobile, IoT, and network systems. Bugcrowd has developed a comprehensive Security Knowledge Platform and has grown significantly, employing over 280 people and maintaining a global community of more than 275,000 security researchers.

The company provides a range of services, including crowdsourced penetration testing, attack surface management, and security risk assessments. Bugcrowd's platform connects ethical hackers with customer programs, enabling continuous testing and remediation. It serves over 600 companies across 30 industries, including government entities like the U.S. Department of Homeland Security. Bugcrowd's mission is to empower organizations at any stage of their security journey, ensuring proactive defense against cyber threats.
companies.lead_investor: Silicon Valley Bank

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.description:
CoreWeave is a cloud computing provider based in Roseland, New Jersey, specializing in GPU-accelerated infrastructure for AI, machine learning, visual effects, rendering, and high-performance computing workloads. Founded in 2017, the company has evolved from its origins in cryptocurrency mining to become North America's largest private cloud provider for high-performance computing. CoreWeave is recognized as NVIDIA's first Elite Cloud Service Provider, granting it early access to advanced GPUs.

The company offers a cloud platform optimized for GPU-intensive workloads, providing accelerated infrastructure for generative AI, rendering services for VFX and animation, and HPC solutions powered by NVIDIA GPUs. CoreWeave supports a diverse range of applications, including real-time AI, drug development, and high-quality content production. With significant funding and a commitment to innovation, CoreWeave emphasizes a culture of curiosity, collaboration, and customer focus.

companies.name: Skild AI
companies.description:
Skild AI is a software development company founded in 2023, based in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, with an additional location in San Francisco, California. The company specializes in creating general-purpose robotic intelligence through its flagship product, the Skild Brain. This AI foundation model is designed to control various robot bodies, adapt to hardware changes in real time, and automate physical tasks in unstructured environments.

Skild AI focuses on enhancing productivity in physical work, targeting sectors such as manufacturing, security, logistics, and construction. The Skild Brain enables robots to perform complex tasks like navigation and dexterous manipulation, making it suitable for applications in dangerous environments, including rescue operations and space exploration. The company has received significant funding, including a $300 million Series A round in 2024, and is backed by notable investors such as Sequoia Capital, Amazon, and NVIDIA.
companies.lead_investor: Nvidia, SoftBank, Macquarie, 1789 Capital

companies.name: Wisedocs
companies.description:
Wisedocs is an AI-powered claims documentation platform based in Toronto, Canada, founded in 2019. The company specializes in enhancing the processing of medical records and complex claims documentation by integrating artificial intelligence with expert human review. Wisedocs processes over 25 million data points monthly and is trained on more than 100 million industry-specific documents.

The platform offers a range of AI-driven solutions, including automated medical record review and summarization, document organization and indexing, claims automation, structured reporting, and collaborative review tools. Wisedocs also features a proprietary Intelligence Engine with 1,500 distinct document type models, ensuring accurate and audit-ready outputs. The company serves various sectors, including insurance carriers, legal firms, independent medical evaluation providers, government agencies, and healthcare organizations, making it a trusted partner in the insurance, healthcare, and legal industries.
companies.lead_investor: CIBC Innovation Banking

companies.name: HumanLayer
companies.description:
HumanLayer is a software company based in San Francisco, founded in 2023 by Dexter Horthy. The company specializes in human-in-the-loop infrastructure for AI agents, focusing on safe and reliable automation in complex workflows. With a team of three employees, HumanLayer offers an API and SDK platform that allows AI agent builders to integrate human intervention for tasks requiring feedback, approvals, and clarifications.

The core platform enables AI agents to contact humans seamlessly across various programming languages and frameworks. Additionally, HumanLayer's flagship product, CodeLayer, is an integrated development environment designed for orchestrating AI coding agents to solve challenging problems in large codebases. The company also provides enterprise services, including tailored workflows and custom integrations, to help organizations scale AI-driven development. Recently, HumanLayer was recognized as a top 4 Enterprise AI startup in Y Combinator's Fall 2024 batch by TechCrunch.
companies.lead_investor: Y Combinator

companies.name: Nvidia
companies.description:
NVIDIA Corporation is a leading American technology company based in Santa Clara, California, founded in 1993 by Jensen Huang, Chris Malachowsky, and Curtis Priem. Initially focused on developing graphics processing units (GPUs) for gaming and multimedia, NVIDIA revolutionized the industry with the launch of the GeForce 256 in 1999, which introduced parallel processing for graphics acceleration. The company has since expanded into accelerated computing, artificial intelligence (AI), and high-performance computing, achieving significant market share in AI training GPUs.

NVIDIA designs a range of products, including GPUs, systems on chips (SoCs), and APIs for various applications. Its GeForce series is well-known in gaming, while Tesla products cater to scientific computing. The company also offers the CUDA platform for parallel computing and the Omniverse platform for virtual collaboration and 3D workflows. NVIDIA's innovations have made it a key player in powering supercomputers and AI models, with partnerships that include major companies like Microsoft, Siemens, and OpenAI.

companies.name: Weco AI
companies.description:
Weco AI is a London-based artificial intelligence company founded in 2023. The company specializes in automating machine learning engineering and AI research and development. With a small team, Weco AI focuses on creating innovative solutions in the data infrastructure and analytics sector.

The company's flagship product, AIDE (AI Assistant for Machine Learning), is an open-source ML engineering agent that excels in generating and optimizing high-quality machine learning code. AIDE automates tasks in data science modeling, deep learning, and AI R&D. Weco AI Functions is an evolution of AIDE, designed as an end-to-end pipeline for building complete solutions. The Weco Platform is a production-grade offering that enables users to build and optimize machine learning pipelines using AIDE ML, a code optimization agent powered by large language models.

Weco AI has successfully raised $8 million in seed funding to enhance its research and development efforts and expand its market presence.

companies.name: TypeSafe AI
companies.description: Make decisions at scale with AI built for systems, not conversation. Process context once, decide in parallel. Boring AI that works.

companies.name: FlyersSoft
companies.description:
Flyers Soft Private Limited is a custom software development and digital transformation company founded in 2019, with headquarters in Chennai, India, and an office in Canton, Michigan, USA. The company employs around 140 people and has reported $1.5 million in revenue for FY25. Flyers Soft specializes in tailored software solutions across more than 20 industries, including healthcare, finance, and education, focusing on creating efficient, growth-oriented products.

The company offers a wide range of services, including application development, mobile app development, UI/UX design, AR/VR metaverse development, cloud engineering, and DevOps. Flyers Soft has completed over 150 projects and maintains a 95% client retention rate. It supports various clients, from startups to Fortune 500 companies, with a commitment to agile processes and long-term relationships. The company emphasizes security and quality, holding certifications such as ISO and HIPAA, and aims to produce world-class technopreneurs through its innovative solutions.

companies.name: Maersk
companies.description:
A.P. Moller - Maersk is a Danish shipping and logistics company founded in 1904. It operates as an integrated container logistics enterprise in 130 countries. The company has a rich history, beginning with the acquisition of a steamship in 1886 and evolving through strategic expansions, including the establishment of its first liner service, Mærsk Line, in 1928.

The company’s primary division, Maersk Line, focuses on container shipping and manages logistics at terminals and ports. A.P. Moller - Maersk also engages in tanker operations, oil and gas, transportation and logistics, and specialty training. The company has expanded internationally since 1919, opening offices in key locations such as New York, Japan, the UK, Thailand, Hong Kong, and Indonesia. The A.P. Moller Foundation, established in 1953, ensures the company’s long-term positive impact on society.

companies.name: Tessl
companies.description:
Tessl is an AI-native development platform that specializes in Spec-Driven Development (SDD). Founded by Guy Podjarny, the company aims to enhance software development by using natural language specifications to guide AI agents in building, maintaining, and evolving reliable software. Tessl has raised $125 million in Series A funding, achieving a valuation of over $500 million. The company operates in the information and document management industry and employs a small team.

Tessl's main offerings include the Tessl Framework, which allows for defining functionality through framework specs and embedded tests, and the Tessl Spec Registry, which provides reusable knowledge packs for consistent guidance across teams. These tools help prevent common issues in AI development, such as hallucinations and regressions, enabling developers to focus on what to build rather than how to implement it. Tessl emphasizes community-driven innovation and aims to support professional builders in achieving speed and reliability in their coding practices.
companies.lead_investor: Accel, Index Ventures

companies.name: Indeed
companies.description:
Indeed is the world's leading job site, founded in 2004 as a job aggregator. It has evolved into an AI-powered marketplace that connects job seekers and employers across over 60 countries. As a subsidiary of Recruit Holdings, Indeed processes around 30 million job listings and collects 320 terabytes of data daily. The platform helps millions of job seekers find opportunities while enabling employers to streamline their hiring processes.

Indeed offers a range of AI-driven tools designed to enhance the hiring experience. These include job search and aggregation services, a one-click application feature, and a pay-for-performance model that charges employers only when job seekers engage. The platform also provides personalized talent matching, resume posting, and company research tools. With a mission-driven culture focused on supporting job seekers, Indeed continues to grow and innovate, making hiring faster and more efficient.

companies.name: Qdrant
companies.description:
Qdrant is an open-source vector database and vector search engine designed for fast and scalable vector similarity search in high-dimensional data. Founded by André Zayarni and Andrey Vasnetsov, the company aims to make vector search technology accessible through both open-source software and scalable cloud services. Qdrant enables organizations to securely leverage proprietary data while maintaining control and privacy.

The primary offering is a high-performance vector database that allows efficient storage, indexing, and searching of billions of vector embeddings. Key features include a vector similarity search engine, Qdrant Cloud for managed services on major cloud platforms, and open-source deployment options. The platform supports advanced capabilities such as hybrid search, advanced filtering, and enterprise-grade security, all built on a reliable Rust foundation. Qdrant powers various AI-driven applications, including semantic search, recommendation systems, and anomaly detection, making it a versatile choice for businesses looking to enhance their data-driven outcomes.
companies.lead_investor: Spark Capital, Atlantic Vantage Point, Bosch Ventures, 42CAP, Unusual Ventures

companies.name: Pinecone
companies.description:
Pinecone is a managed, cloud-native vector database founded in 2019. It enables developers to build, scale, and deploy AI applications through fast vector search and similarity matching via a simple API. The company aims to simplify AI infrastructure by providing essential storage and retrieval solutions, allowing users to focus on application development without worrying about algorithm selection or performance tuning.

The flagship product is a serverless vector database optimized for storing and querying high-dimensional vector embeddings. Key features include real-time indexing, performant algorithms for approximate nearest neighbor search, and tiered storage for scalability. Pinecone supports a variety of applications, such as semantic search and recommendation systems, and is designed for mission-critical generative AI workloads.

Pinecone serves a diverse range of clients, from startups to Fortune 500 companies, including notable names like Shopify and Vanguard. The company operates with a usage-based pricing model, accommodating users with varying needs, from small teams to large enterprises. Pinecone is committed to reliability and security, offering a 99.95% uptime SLA and holding multiple industry certifications.
companies.lead_investor: ICONIQ Growth, Wing Venture Capital, Menlo Ventures, Andreessen Horowitz

companies.name: Snyk
companies.description:
Snyk Limited is a developer-first cybersecurity company founded in 2015, with its headquarters in Boston and additional offices in Tel Aviv, London, Ottawa, and Zurich. The company focuses on integrating security into developer workflows through its Cloud Native Application Security Platform. Snyk employs around 1,400 people and achieved unicorn status in 2020, with significant revenue growth projected to exceed $300 million in 2025.

Snyk offers a range of products designed to identify and fix vulnerabilities throughout the software development lifecycle. Key offerings include Snyk Open Source for managing open-source dependencies, Snyk Container for securing container images, Snyk Infrastructure as Code (IaC) for cloud infrastructure configurations, and Snyk Code for static application security testing. The company emphasizes developer-friendly tools and has formed partnerships with various developer tool providers to enhance security integration in workflows. Snyk serves over 700 paying customers, including notable users like Smartsheet.
companies.lead_investor: ServiceNow

companies.name: Neo4j
companies.description:
Neo4j, Inc. is a software company based in San Mateo, California, and Malmö, Sweden. It specializes in developing the Neo4j graph database management system, which is designed for storing, processing, and querying highly connected data. The database uses nodes, edges, and attributes to represent complex relationships, making it particularly effective for analyzing data in the context of AI and cloud transformations.

Founded by CEO Emil Eifrem, Neo4j has seen significant growth since its initial release in 2007, with major updates released over the years. The company has raised substantial funding, including a $325 million Series F round in 2021, which has helped it achieve a valuation of over $2 billion. Neo4j emphasizes its role in the AI ecosystem, providing clarity on relationships to support practical AI applications. Its flagship product is ACID-compliant and offers high availability, making it suitable for enterprise-scale deployments.
companies.lead_investor: Noteus Partners

companies.name: The Carlyle Group
companies.description:
The Carlyle Group is a global alternative asset management firm founded in 1987 in Washington, D.C. by David M. Rubenstein and others. It specializes in private equity, leveraged buyouts, growth capital, real assets, global credit, and related financial services. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., Carlyle has grown significantly, managing $435 billion in assets across over 600 investment vehicles and employing approximately 2,200 people in 28-29 offices worldwide.

Carlyle offers a range of services, including private equity and financial solutions, with expertise in sectors such as energy, technology, healthcare, and defense. The firm focuses on buyouts, strategic investments, and portfolio diversification, leveraging a collaborative approach to create value. Its notable investments span various industries, including defense, consumer media, and real estate, reflecting its commitment to product diversity and ethical operations.

companies.name: Clay
companies.description:
Clay is a sales and marketing technology company based in Brooklyn, New York, founded in 2017. The company offers an AI-powered platform designed for data enrichment, outbound marketing automation, and growth workflows. Clay helps teams identify, research, and engage high-intent customers by aggregating data from over 100 premium sources, including public databases and social media.

The platform streamlines revenue operations by automating personalized outbound campaigns and lead generation. It allows users to conduct complex growth experiments that traditionally required larger teams, significantly reducing manual research efforts. Clay's services include real-time intent data tracking, multi-channel lead sourcing, and seamless CRM integration. The company supports a range of clients, from solo founders to large enterprises, with tiered pricing plans and a 14-day trial option. Notable clients include OpenAI and Canva, who have benefited from enhanced data-driven outbound strategies.
companies.lead_investor: Maple VC, Meritech Capital, First Round Capital, Sequoia, BoxGroup, CapitalG, Boldstart Ventures

companies.name: Figma
companies.description:
Figma is a public American software company founded in 2012, specializing in a cloud-based collaborative design platform. Headquartered in San Francisco, Figma enables real-time creation, prototyping, and iteration of user interfaces directly in web browsers. The platform supports vector graphics, multiplayer collaboration, and developer handoff, making it a comprehensive tool for designers and developers alike.

Figma's journey began with the founders' vision to simplify creative tools for web use. It launched its public version in 2016 and quickly gained popularity due to its innovative features and freemium model. The company has made significant strides in the design space, including the introduction of Figma Community for sharing files and the acquisition of AI startup Weavy to enhance its offerings with advanced editing capabilities. With approximately 2,425 employees, Figma continues to lead in the digital product design market, serving a diverse range of global teams and organizations.

companies.name: Notion
companies.description: Notion is a California-based collaborative workspace platform that provides solutions such as document processing and knowledge management for businesses.
companies.lead_investor: GIC, Sequoia Capital, Index Ventures

companies.name: Meticulous
companies.description:
Meticulous is an automated frontend testing platform based in San Francisco, California, with a registered office in London, England. Founded in 2021 and backed by Y Combinator, the company has around 5 employees. Meticulous specializes in generating and maintaining visual end-to-end tests using AI, eliminating the need for developers to write or manage test code manually.

The platform operates through a three-step process: record, simulate, and test. It records user sessions from applications and replays them against new code versions, quickly delivering results. Key features include AI-generated test suites that cover all code branches and user flows, an augmented Chromium browser for efficient session replay, and automatic network mocking for side-effect-free tests. Meticulous's solution integrates seamlessly with CI/CD pipelines, allowing for continuous testing with minimal setup. The company is dedicated to enhancing the software development process for front-end developers by providing tools that support robust and reliable software creation.

companies.name: Y Combinator
companies.description:
Y Combinator (YC) is a prominent startup accelerator based in Silicon Valley, founded in 2005. It provides seed funding, mentorship, and resources to early-stage startups, having supported over 5,000 companies with a combined valuation exceeding $800 billion. Initially established in Cambridge, Massachusetts, YC moved to Silicon Valley in 2006 to leverage a larger network of investors and startups. The program runs twice a year, selecting hundreds of startups each cycle and welcoming founders from various backgrounds and ages.

YC offers a range of resources to its startups, including access to a vast investor network, a private social platform for alumni, and tools for team building. Notable platforms include "Work at a Startup," which connects candidates with job opportunities at YC companies, and "Hacker News," a forum for discussions and job postings. The YC Library provides curated content to help founders navigate the startup landscape. Successful companies like Airbnb, Dropbox, and Stripe have emerged from YC, showcasing the accelerator's impact on the startup ecosystem.

companies.name: Etched
companies.description:
Etched (Etched.ai, Inc.) is a semiconductor startup founded in 2022 by Harvard dropouts Gavin Uberti, Chris Zhu, and Robert Wachen. Based in Cupertino, California, the company specializes in application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs) designed for transformer-based AI models. Their technology aims to outperform general-purpose GPUs in inference tasks, enabling faster and more cost-effective processing for large language models (LLMs).

The flagship product, Sohu, is the first chip specifically built for transformer AI architecture. It is fabricated on TSMC's 4nm process and features 144 GB of HBM3E memory per chip. Sohu optimizes matrix multiplication patterns for transformer inference, achieving significantly higher performance compared to traditional GPU systems. Etched also offers complete server systems that integrate Sohu chips and software, facilitating easy model porting and scalability for advanced AI workloads. The company targets hyperscalers, AI research firms, and enterprises that require robust solutions for real-time applications and large-scale data processing.
companies.lead_investor: Stripes, Ribbit Capital, Peter Thiel, Positive Sum

companies.name: Fireworks
companies.description:
Fireworks AI is a high-performance inference platform founded in 2022, designed to help developers and enterprises run, fine-tune, and deploy open-source large language and image generation models. The company was established by former leaders from PyTorch, Meta, and Google, with CEO Lin Qiao having played a key role in the development of PyTorch.

The platform offers a range of capabilities, including serverless inference for instant access to popular models, on-demand GPU infrastructure that scales with production needs, and advanced model fine-tuning techniques. Fireworks AI supports state-of-the-art open-source models like Stable Diffusion XL and Llama 2, and provides deployment options through software-as-a-service hosting or containerized solutions in customer virtual private clouds. The infrastructure is built on globally distributed virtual cloud technology, ensuring enterprise-grade security and reliability.
companies.lead_investor: Lightspeed Venture Partners, Sequoia, Evantic Capital, Index Ventures

companies.name: Oxylabs
companies.description:
Oxylabs.io is a Lithuania-based web intelligence collection platform and premium proxy service provider, established in 2015. The company specializes in ethical public web data extraction, offering a wide range of tools for reliable data gathering. With a workforce of around 500 employees, Oxylabs generates approximately $121.9 million in revenue and serves over 4,000 clients globally across various industries, including e-commerce, cybersecurity, travel, IT, and hospitality.

The company provides extensive proxy solutions, featuring one of the largest networks with over 177 million residential IPs and 2 million datacenter IPs. Their offerings include Scraper APIs with AI-driven features, pre-built datasets for various applications, and industry-specific solutions tailored to meet the needs of sectors like e-commerce and cybersecurity. Oxylabs is recognized for its commitment to ethical practices and innovation, being a founding member of the Ethical Web Data Collection Initiative and consistently ranked as Europe’s fastest-growing web data collection company by the Financial Times.

companies.name: Restate
companies.description:
Restate is a lightweight, open-source workflows-as-code platform that simplifies the development of distributed applications. Founded by creators of Apache Flink, Restate launched its version 1.0 and a managed cloud service in 2024 after securing $7 million in seed funding. The platform is designed to enable developers to create resilient distributed apps, AI agents, and orchestration systems that automatically handle failures.

Restate offers three optimized service types: Basic Service, Virtual Object, and Workflow. Each service type leverages durable execution to ensure reliable request completion with fault tolerance. The platform supports deployment flexibility, allowing for self-hosting or use of Restate Cloud, and integrates seamlessly with various environments like servers, containers, and serverless functions. Developers can write services in their preferred programming languages and benefit from features such as high concurrency and state management, making it suitable for a range of applications from e-commerce to complex workflows.
companies.lead_investor: Guy Fighel, Shawn Wang, First Minute Capital, Monica Sarbu, Jay Kreps, Aris Kyriakos Koliopoulos, Essence Venture Capital, Jan Sessenhausen, Olivier Pomel, Andrew Nest, Neha Narkhede, Ameet Patel, Redpoint, Julia Schottenstein, Roopak Venkatakrishnan, Chris Shagen

companies.name: commercetools
companies.description:
commercetools is a Munich-based enterprise software company founded in 2006. It specializes in a cloud-native, composable commerce platform built on MACH principles, which stands for modular, API-first, cloud-native, and headless. This platform enables large organizations to create scalable and customizable digital commerce experiences.

The company has pioneered headless and composable commerce, allowing enterprises to achieve real-time adaptability and scalability across various channels and business models. Its core offering includes features such as ultra-fast performance for high-volume operations, a modular architecture for custom integrations, and predictable order-based pricing. commercetools serves a diverse range of industries, including retail, fashion, grocery, telecom, automotive, manufacturing, and healthcare, supporting both B2B and B2C models.
companies.lead_investor: commercetools

companies.name: Elastic
companies.description:
Elastic (Elastic N.V.) is a prominent search and analytics company known for its Elastic Stack, an open-source platform that supports search, observability, security, and AI applications for enterprises globally. The company specializes in technology that enables real-time search, analysis, and visualization of large volumes of structured and unstructured data, catering to both consumer and enterprise needs.

The core of the Elastic Stack includes Elasticsearch, a distributed search and analytics engine, along with Logstash and Beats for data ingestion, and Kibana for visualization. This platform is designed for high scalability and resilience, offering deployment options on-premises, in public or private clouds, and as serverless solutions. Elastic serves a diverse range of industries, including technology, healthcare, and finance, and is trusted by many leading organizations worldwide. Its offerings include search solutions, observability tools, security features, and a versatile Search AI Platform that integrates generative AI and machine learning capabilities.

companies.name: Circle
companies.description:
Circle is a financial technology company based in Boston, Massachusetts, founded in October 2013 by Jeremy Allaire and Sean Neville. The company focuses on creating a new internet financial system that facilitates global money movement using blockchain technology and digital currencies. Circle is publicly traded on the NYSE under the ticker CRCL and was acquired by Ripple in May 2025.

Circle offers a range of products and services centered around stablecoins and blockchain infrastructure. Key offerings include USDC, a regulated stablecoin pegged to the US dollar, and various payment solutions like the Circle Account for secure fund custody and global payments. The company also provides developer tools, smart contracts, and an open Layer-1 blockchain called Arc, supporting operations across more than 20 blockchains. Circle serves a diverse clientele, including financial institutions, businesses, developers, and consumers, operating in over 185 countries with a strong emphasis on regulatory compliance and security.

companies.name: Audible
companies.description:
Audible is a prominent digital audiobook and spoken audio content platform, founded in 1995 by Donald Katz and Tim Mott. Based in Newark, New Jersey, the company offers subscription-based access to a vast library of audiobooks, exclusive original content, and podcasts. Audible became part of Amazon in 2008.

The company launched its first product, the Audible MobilePlayer, in 1997, marking a significant innovation in portable digital audio. Audible introduced its subscription model in 2000, allowing customers to access its extensive catalog for a flat monthly fee. The platform has expanded internationally, serving audiences in countries like Germany, the UK, France, and Australia. 

Audible's offerings include a comprehensive audiobook library, original audio content produced by Audible Studios, a curated selection of podcasts, and the Audiobook Creation Exchange (ACX), which supports independent creators in producing and distributing audiobooks. In 2024, listeners consumed over 5.4 billion hours of content on the platform, highlighting its global reach and diverse audience.
companies.lead_investor: Amazon

companies.name: Yugabyte
companies.description:
Yugabyte is a company based in Sunnyvale, California, founded in 2016 by former Facebook engineers. The company specializes in developing YugabyteDB, an open-source distributed SQL database designed for cloud-native applications. YugabyteDB combines the relational capabilities of SQL with the scalability and resilience of NoSQL, making it suitable for modern transactional applications.

YugabyteDB is built on PostgreSQL source code and offers high availability, ensuring that applications can withstand outages. It enhances PostgreSQL compatibility, allowing a wide range of Postgres applications to run in distributed environments. The company has achieved significant growth, employing around 350 people and reaching a valuation of $1.3 billion by 2021 after raising substantial funding. YugabyteDB is available in both community and enterprise editions, with commercial features fully open-sourced since July 2019.
companies.lead_investor: Wipro Ventures, Lightspeed Venture Partners, Alkeon Capital, Meritech Capital, Wells Fargo Securities, Sapphire Ventures, 8VC, Dell Technologies Capital

companies.name: Docusign
companies.description:
DocuSign, Inc. is a prominent provider of cloud-based electronic signature and digital transaction management solutions. Founded in 2003 in Seattle, the company has transformed the way organizations handle agreements by replacing paper-based processes with secure, legally compliant digital alternatives. Its core offering, the eSignature solution, allows users to sign documents securely from anywhere, while the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform enhances this by automating and analyzing agreements using data, AI, and machine learning.

Headquartered in San Francisco, DocuSign serves over 1.5 million paying customers and more than a billion users across 180+ countries. The company has made significant investments in research and development, totaling over $300 million since its inception. With a focus on sustainability and a commitment to innovation, DocuSign continues to expand its capabilities, including cloud-based document storage and remote online notarization, making it a valuable partner for businesses of all sizes.

companies.name: Airtable
companies.description:
Airtable is a cloud-based collaboration platform based in San Francisco, California. Founded in 2012, it combines the simplicity of spreadsheets with the power of databases, allowing users to organize, track, and manage work without coding. The platform supports no-code app development and offers features like customizable data fields, multiple views (grid, calendar, kanban), and workflow automation.

Since its public launch in 2015, Airtable has grown significantly, serving over 500,000 organizations, including 80% of the Fortune 100. It employs a freemium model, converting free users to paid subscribers through continuous feature updates. The platform is designed for scalability, catering to teams from startups to large enterprises, and emphasizes creativity through community-driven templates and integrations with tools like Salesforce and SAP.
companies.lead_investor: Salesforce Ventures, Franklin Templeton Investments, Benchmark, D1 Capital Partners, Coatue Management, DFO Management, Thrive Capital, XN

companies.name: The Browser Company
companies.description:
The Browser Company is a software company dedicated to enhancing the internet experience through its innovative web browser. Founded with a mission to evoke positive emotions, the company values creativity, collaboration, and exploration. Its team dynamics are inspired by road trip analogies, emphasizing respect for diverse opinions and creating environments that foster inspired work.

The primary product is a web browser designed to improve user interaction with the internet. The company aims to create subtle, meaningful experiences that resonate with users, positioning this browser as the foundation for future innovations in internet usage. The close-knit team works together to capture insights and celebrate successes, reflecting their commitment to quality and shared values.
companies.lead_investor: Atlassian

companies.name: GitHub
companies.description:
GitHub is a web-based platform for version control, collaboration, and software development. It allows developers to share, edit, and store programming code online. Founded in 2007 and launched in 2008, GitHub has grown to over 150 million users and hosts more than 420 million projects. The platform is owned by Microsoft, which acquired it in 2018.

GitHub offers a range of services, including repository hosting, version control, code review, issue tracking, and project management. Users can create their own copies of repositories, track project changes, and host web pages through GitHub Pages. GitHub Enterprise provides secure on-premises repository hosting for large teams. Additionally, GitHub Copilot, an AI-powered developer tool, significantly contributes to the company's revenue growth. Major companies like Google, Facebook, and Microsoft utilize GitHub for their development needs. The company is headquartered in San Francisco.

companies.name: Oracle
companies.description:
Oracle Corporation is a global leader in enterprise software and cloud computing, founded in 1977. Headquartered in Austin, Texas, with a significant campus in Redwood City, California, Oracle pioneered relational database management systems (RDBMS) and has grown to offer a wide range of database technology, business applications, cloud services, and AI solutions. The company reported annual revenues of $42.44 billion in 2022 and employs around 143,000 people.

Oracle's product portfolio includes over 70 solutions, with its flagship Oracle Database being the first commercial RDBMS to utilize SQL for efficient data management. The company provides enterprise applications such as ERP and CRM, as well as cloud infrastructure and AI integrations. Oracle serves various sectors, including finance, healthcare, and government, delivering reliable and scalable solutions for large-scale data operations.

companies.name: QuantumBlack, AI by McKinsey
companies.description:
McKinsey & Company is a prominent global management consulting firm founded in 1926 by James O. McKinsey. Originally established in Chicago, the firm has grown to become one of the largest consulting firms in the world, known for its expertise in advising corporations and governments on strategic and operational challenges. McKinsey has a rich history of innovation, including pioneering the use of accounting principles as management tools and playing a key role in the development of the universal product code (UPC).

The firm offers a range of services, including strategic consulting, operational improvement, and organizational design. Over the years, McKinsey has expanded its capabilities to include analytics, digital consulting, and sustainability initiatives. It partners with clients across various sectors, from multinational corporations to government agencies, and is committed to social impact through initiatives like the nonprofit Generation, which addresses global youth unemployment. McKinsey is recognized for its strong professional culture and values, focusing on attracting top talent to tackle complex problems.

companies.name: InsightSciences.ai
companies.description:
Insight Sciences partners with global brands to transform how they understand and engage with their audiences. Our groundbreaking platform delivers dynamic, scalable, and bias-free insights, enabling businesses to innovate and adapt at the speed of culture. We are disrupting consumer insights and market research.

We take on only the most meaningful challenges, designing solutions that shape industries and drive growth. Insight Sciences is where creativity, science, and technology converge to define the next era of market research. Our relationships are confidential, spanning industries from consumer goods to technology.

We are a team of seasoned technologists, marketers, and entrepreneurs who have founded some of the largest advertising agencies, influenced product development at the world's leading platforms, and collaborated with over 30% of the most iconic global brands. With expertise spanning machine learning, applied mathematics, and behavioral economics, we build systems that unlock future-focused strategies and deliver measurable impact.

companies.name: Qodo
companies.description:
Qodo is an AI code review platform that assists engineering teams in maintaining code quality and accelerating development workflows. Founded in 2022 by Itamar Friedman and Dedy Kredo, the company has grown to employ 100 people across Israel, the U.S., and Europe. Qodo has received recognition from major media outlets and was named among TechCrunch's AI Disruptors 60 list.

The platform offers a unified solution that includes AI code review, code quality management, and software development lifecycle governance. Key features include a Git plugin for automated pull request suggestions, an IDE plugin for real-time coding assistance, and a CLI plugin for command-line workflows. Qodo's technology integrates with popular platforms like GitHub and GitLab, providing capabilities such as compliance checks, bug detection, and automated issue resolution. Notable enterprise customers include Monday.com, Ford, and Intuit, with significant productivity improvements reported by users.
companies.lead_investor: Susa Ventures, Qumra Capital, Square Peg Capital, TLV Partners, Peter Welinder, Clara Shih, Maor Investments, Vine Ventures, S Ventures

companies.name: Daytona
companies.description:
Daytona is an open-source platform that provides secure and scalable infrastructure for running AI-generated code and agent workflows. Founded in 2023 by the creators of Codeanywhere, Daytona initially focused on self-hosted development environment management for enterprises. In 2025, the company pivoted to AI agent infrastructure, launching Daytona Cloud, which offers purpose-built runtimes for AI agents while maintaining its core development environment orchestration capabilities.

The main product is a development environment manager that allows for rapid provisioning of secure, stateful environments optimized for AI workflows. Key features include fast sandbox spin-up times, isolated runtime protection, and support for stateful operations like databases and automated reports. Daytona supports various deployment options, including self-hosted, air-gapped, and cloud-based solutions. The platform is designed to enhance developer productivity and is open-source, ensuring transparency and security. Daytona serves large enterprises and AI agent users, with Sentry recognized as a key customer.
companies.lead_investor: Datadog, Upfront Ventures, FirstMark, Pace Capital, Figma Ventures, Darkmode Ventures, e2vc

companies.name: Legora
companies.description:
Legora is a legal tech company based in Stockholm, founded in 2023. It provides an AI-powered collaborative workspace designed for lawyers and law firms. The platform enhances document review, research, drafting, and advisory tasks while automating repetitive work. Legora combines human judgment with AI capabilities to streamline legal workflows, making it easier for legal professionals to manage their tasks efficiently.

The company offers an integrated AI workspace built on Microsoft Azure OpenAI Service. Key features include document and tabular review for M&A analysis, legal research capabilities, and support for drafting and workflows. Legora also provides add-ins for Word and Outlook, facilitating seamless integration into existing processes. With a focus on security and compliance, Legora adheres to rigorous standards, including ISO certifications and GDPR compliance. 

Legora operates in over 30 markets and has a growing team of more than 580 coworkers. Its mission is to empower lawyers by fostering collaboration and innovation in legal work, ultimately setting a new standard in the industry.
companies.lead_investor: Adams Street Partners, Bain Capital, Barclays, Bessemer Venture Partners, Insight Partners, Redpoint, General Catalyst, ICONIQ Capital, Y Combinator, AirTree Ventures, Liberty Global, Menlo Ventures, FirstMark, Salesforce Ventures, Atlassian, Sands Capital Management, Starwood Capital Group, Geodesic Capital, Nikesh Arora, Alkeon Capital, Accel, Benchmark, NVentures

companies.name: HeyGen
companies.description:
HeyGen is an AI-powered video generation and translation platform that helps businesses and individuals create professional-quality videos quickly and affordably. The company focuses on enhancing video content production through generative AI, offering over 120 AI avatars and 300 customizable voices. Users can produce videos by inputting text, images, or audio, and the platform supports multilingual dubbing in over 175 languages, ensuring effective global content distribution.

With a diverse customer base of over 100,000 teams, HeyGen serves various sectors, including marketing, sales, online education, and nonprofit storytelling. The platform allows for brand customization and interactive video content creation, making it versatile for different business needs. HeyGen also offers an enterprise solution with features tailored for large organizations, including unlimited video creation, robust security, and personalized support. The company has achieved significant growth, reaching profitability in 2023 and surpassing $35 million in annual recurring revenue.
companies.lead_investor: BOND, Benchmark, Thrive Capital, Conviction VC, SV Angel

companies.name: Apify
companies.description:
Apify is a cloud platform that specializes in web scraping, browser automation, and data extraction for AI applications. It features the Apify Store, a marketplace with over 10,000 ready-made automation tools, known as Actors. These Actors can be deployed, customized, and scaled to collect real-time web data for various applications, including AI training, social media monitoring, and competitive intelligence.

The platform allows users to run Actors without coding, offering features like JavaScript rendering, IP rotation, and integrations with popular tools. Apify also provides custom development services, including end-to-end web scraping and automation solutions, supported by a dedicated team. With a focus on enterprise needs, Apify ensures compliance with data protection regulations and offers robust infrastructure for data processing. Pricing starts at $49 per month, with scalable options based on usage. Apify serves over 15,000 customers globally, processing significant amounts of data monthly.
companies.lead_investor: J&T Ventures

companies.name: Ufonia
companies.description:
Ufonia Limited is a digital health company based in Oxford, established in 2017. The company specializes in Artificial Clinical Intelligence, focusing on automating routine clinical conversations through AI and clinical evidence. Ufonia aims to enhance healthcare accessibility and affordability, improving patient convenience and freeing clinicians for more critical interactions.

The flagship product, Dora, is a medically regulated autonomous clinical assistant that conducts natural voice conversations with patients for routine consultations, such as post-operative cataract assessments. Dora is designed to support scalable patient outreach and is backed by clinical studies that ensure safety and quality. Ufonia collaborates with various partners, including Health Innovation Oxford and Thames Valley, and has received funding from Innovate UK and NHS/NIHR.

Ufonia serves multiple NHS sites across the UK and has expanded its reach internationally, including partnerships in Europe, Hong Kong, and the United States. The company is committed to addressing healthcare challenges through a diverse team of professionals guided by values of boldness, collaboration, and empathy.
companies.lead_investor: Wavemaker Three-Sixty Health, Octopus Ventures

companies.name: Arize
companies.description:
Arize AI is a machine learning observability platform founded in 2020 and based in Mill Valley, California. The company focuses on monitoring, diagnosing, debugging, and improving the performance of AI models and applications in production, including large language models and multimodal systems. Arize addresses the challenges of AI transparency by providing tools that enhance visibility into model reasoning, data drift, and performance issues, ensuring reliability and scalability in AI deployments.

The flagship platform, Arize AX, offers enterprise-grade observability features such as real-time monitoring, evaluation, debugging, and optimization tools. These capabilities help AI engineers manage model behavior, track performance regressions, and ensure service level objectives are met. Arize serves major enterprises like Uber, Klaviyo, and PepsiCo, supporting a range of applications from job recommendation systems to generative AI projects. The company emphasizes open-source standards and interoperability, with its open-source offering, Arize Phoenix, achieving over two million monthly downloads.
companies.lead_investor: Adams Street Partners, Battery Ventures, Datadog, TCV, Foundation Capital, M12, Industry Ventures, OMERS Ventures, SineWave Ventures, PagerDuty, Swift Ventures, Archerman Capital

companies.name: PayPal
companies.description:
PayPal is a global financial technology company that has been enhancing commerce for over 25 years. It enables secure and personalized money movement, shopping, and selling across approximately 200 markets. Originally founded as Confinity, Inc. in 1998, PayPal launched its core product in 1999, allowing users to send money via email using bank accounts and credit cards. The company focuses on inclusion, innovation, collaboration, and wellness, aiming to return more money to customers quickly through its services.

PayPal offers a variety of payment and financial services for consumers, merchants, and businesses. Its core payment solutions allow secure online transactions, while business tools provide a unified platform for payment acceptance and operational efficiency. The company also offers working capital loans, transaction management features, and additional tools like a PayPal ATM/debit card and developer APIs for integrations. Through PayPal Ventures, the company invests in startups across various sectors, supporting innovation in fintech and commerce.

companies.name: Prefect
companies.description:
Prefect Technologies, Inc. is an open-source workflow orchestration platform founded in 2018 and based in Washington, D.C. The company provides a unified automation engine for data, machine learning, agents, and background tasks. Its platform allows teams to build, manage, and observe complex pipelines with minimal engineering overhead.

Prefect's offerings include Prefect Core, an open-source self-hosted orchestration tool, and Prefect Cloud, a fully managed service that features zero maintenance and autoscaling. The platform supports various use cases, such as data science, machine learning, and data engineering, ensuring reliable workflows and efficient resource management. Key features include event-driven triggers, observability tools for visual debugging, and integrations with popular services like dbt and Snowflake. Prefect emphasizes a code-as-workflows approach, enabling users to define flows and tasks in Python without rewriting code.
companies.lead_investor: Bessemer Venture Partners, Tiger Global, Positive Sum

companies.name: Hornet.dev
companies.description: Agents need a new retrieval engine: HORNET.dev

companies.name: The New York Times Games
companies.description:
The New York Times is a prestigious daily newspaper founded in 1851, known for its commitment to high-quality journalism. It has evolved from a penny press publication into a global media leader, focusing on comprehensive news coverage, investigative reporting, and digital innovation.
