The newspaper operates under the slogan "All the News That's Fit to Print," reflecting its dedication to integrity in reporting.

The New York Times offers daily editions that include international news, business coverage, and various sections such as fashion, arts, and books. It introduced a digital subscription model in 2011, which has significantly increased its readership and revenue. The company also has a history of innovations, including being the first major U.S. paper to target business readers and pioneering automated printing. With a long-standing global distribution, The New York Times continues to uphold its principles of impartial coverage across print and digital platforms.

companies.name: egghead.io
companies.description:
egghead.io is an e-learning platform that offers high-quality, concise video courses on web development tools and technologies. Founded by full-stack developer Joel Hooks, the company operates remotely with a team of 11-50 employees based in Vancouver, Washington. The platform is designed to provide bite-sized, information-dense lessons that respect users' time, focusing on practical knowledge in areas like React, Next.js, and Chrome Dev Tools.

The core offering includes video screencasts and courses created by expert contributors, featuring a large library of filterable lessons. Users can access both free and Pro content, with features such as bookmarking, ratings, and completion tracking. egghead.io utilizes a modern tech stack, including Next.js and Amazon Web Services, to deliver its content efficiently. The platform primarily serves individual developers and tech-savvy learners looking for targeted web development education.

companies.name: Dioxus Labs
companies.description:
Dioxus Labs develops and maintains the Dioxus framework, a fullstack, cross-platform app framework built in Rust. This framework allows developers to create user interfaces and applications for web, desktop, mobile, and more using a single codebase. Dioxus aims to provide a high-performance alternative to existing frameworks like React and Flutter, emphasizing type-safe server/client communication and rapid deployment features.

The core offering includes the Dioxus framework, which features a React-like UI library, reactive stores, and components for state management. Additionally, Dioxus Labs provides a command-line interface (CLI) for development, along with tools and resources such as a VSCode extension and comprehensive documentation. The company fosters community engagement through weekly office hours and maintains an open-source presence on GitHub, supporting Rust developers and teams looking for efficient cross-platform solutions.
companies.lead_investor: Y Combinator

companies.name: Coinbase
companies.description:
Coinbase is a financial technology and services company based in San Francisco, California. Founded in June 2012 by Brian Armstrong and Fred Ehrsam, it operates a secure cryptocurrency exchange platform. Coinbase went public through a direct listing on NASDAQ in April 2021 and trades under the ticker COIN. The company's mission is to enhance economic freedom for over 1 billion people by modernizing the financial system and providing a reliable platform for engaging with crypto assets.

Coinbase offers a wide range of cryptocurrency services, including trading, secure digital wallets, staking opportunities, custodial services, and fast global transfers. The platform supports over 250 major digital assets, such as Bitcoin, Ethereum, Dogecoin, and Litecoin. With a diverse customer base that includes retail and institutional investors, as well as over 245,000 ecosystem partners in more than 100 countries, Coinbase emphasizes identity verification and financial compliance to ensure account security and digital asset protection.

companies.name: Venice AI
companies.description:
Venice.ai is a generative AI platform founded in 2024 by Erik Voorhees, focusing on user privacy and decentralization. The platform offers uncensored text, image, and code generation, along with API services, all while ensuring that user data is stored locally in their browser. This approach allows users to engage with AI tools without concerns about data retention or surveillance.

The platform provides a range of features, including AI chat, image generation, document analysis, and advanced tools for text editing and character creation. Users can access Venice.ai through a web or mobile app, benefiting from a responsive experience. The service is designed for non-technical users seeking a straightforward alternative to existing AI tools, with options for both free and pro plans that cater to different needs. Privacy is a core principle, with all content encrypted and processed without server storage, making it a suitable choice for privacy-conscious individuals and developers.

companies.name: Skydio
companies.description:
Skydio is a leading U.S. drone manufacturer based in San Mateo, California, specializing in autonomous flight technology. Founded in 2014 by MIT graduates Adam Bry, Abe Bachrach, and Matt Donahoe, the company designs and produces intelligent drones powered by advanced AI. Skydio aims to make autonomous drone systems more affordable, significantly reducing costs through the use of standard smartphone components.

The company offers a range of products, including the Skydio R1, Skydio 2, Skydio X2, and Skydio Dock, along with 3D Scan software for inspections. Skydio serves over 2,000 enterprise and government organizations across various sectors, including military, public safety, energy, and construction. With a strong focus on AI-driven autonomous navigation, Skydio's drones are known for their ability to navigate complex environments and capture high-quality footage without expert piloting. The company has shipped more than 40,000 drones and continues to manufacture thousands of systems annually in California.

companies.name: Cloudflare
companies.description:
Cloudflare, Inc. is a global cloud platform company founded in 2009, headquartered in Palo Alto, California. The company specializes in internet infrastructure services that enhance website and application security, performance, and reliability. With an extensive edge network that spans over 300 cities in more than 120 countries, Cloudflare protects over 20% of the world's websites, including more than eight million websites, APIs, and applications.

Cloudflare's mission is to build a better Internet by providing a comprehensive cloud platform that includes a reverse proxy, content delivery network (CDN) caching, DDoS mitigation, bot filtering, and web application firewall (WAF). Its services are designed to make the internet faster, safer, and more reliable. The company also offers developer tools, such as serverless computing at the edge and APIs for custom applications, along with network analytics and insights. Cloudflare serves businesses of all sizes globally, handling DNS for nearly 100,000 of the Alexa top 1 million sites.

companies.name: DX
companies.description:
DX is a developer intelligence platform that helps engineering leaders measure productivity and enhance developer experience. Founded by Abi Noda and Greyson Junggren, the platform integrates quantitative metrics from engineering tools with qualitative insights from developers to provide actionable data.

Key features of DX include Direct Benchmarking™, which allows companies to compare their performance against peers, and Experience Data, which captures self-reported metrics to identify workflow bottlenecks. The platform also offers DX AI for automating insights and generating custom reports, along with tools to analyze developer experiences and track AI transformation. Notable customers like Vercel, Brex, and Pfizer have achieved significant productivity gains using DX.

The leadership team includes experts in the field, supported by notable investors from the tech industry. The platform emphasizes scientific rigor, drawing on research from its Chief Scientist and Head of Research.
companies.lead_investor: Atlassian

companies.name: LatchBio
companies.description:
LatchBio is a biotechnology company based in San Francisco, founded in 2021. It offers a modular, programmable data infrastructure platform designed to enhance biopharma research and development. The platform connects bench scientists, computational scientists, IT teams, and R&D leadership, facilitating collaboration and efficiency in biocomputing.

The core offerings of LatchBio include tools for data visualization, analysis computing, and workflow centralization. It provides white-labeled analysis software that allows solution providers to quickly launch branded portals with custom pipelines. The platform also features no-code and low-code tools for biologists, enabling easy access to bioinformatics resources. LatchBio emphasizes data centralization, security, and compliance, utilizing technologies like Flyte for workflow orchestration to meet the growing demands of biotech data. The company serves a range of clients, including Curio Biosciences and AtlasXOmics, and collaborates with partners like Miraomics and Pythia Biosciences to advance its offerings.

companies.name: Antigravity
companies.description:
Wiring mind & mode

companies.name: AlixPartners
companies.description:
AlixPartners is a global consulting firm founded in 1981, headquartered in New York City. The firm specializes in high-impact advisory services for urgent corporate challenges, including restructuring, turnarounds, performance improvement, litigation consulting, forensic accounting, and IT/information management. With over 3,500 professionals across 25 offices worldwide, AlixPartners serves a diverse range of clients, including Fortune 500 and middle-market companies.

Originally recognized for its innovation in the restructuring industry, particularly in the automotive sector, AlixPartners has evolved into a full-service advisory firm. The company has played a significant role in major Chapter 11 reorganizations, advising clients like Kmart, General Motors, and Enron. AlixPartners is committed to responsible business practices and sustainability, aiming to make a positive impact during critical moments for its clients.

companies.name: AIDAChip
companies.description:
Chip design is one of the most complex multiplayer engineering challenges in the world. Dozens of disciplines — analog, digital, verification, layout, signoff — all building different pieces of the same silicon. All interdependent.

  ▎ The tools are working. The system isn't.

Only 14% of chips succeed on first silicon. Roughly 70% of failures trace back to specification misalignment between teams — not engineering error. Engineers spend 70% of their time on workflow friction and coordination, leaving only 30% for innovation. The bottleneck is no longer physics. It's alignment at scale.

Every AI tool in chip design today is single-player: one engineer, one session, one context. But chip design is irreducibly multiplayer. A single spec change creates obligations across five disciplines simultaneously. Individual acceleration is not the same as system coherence.

AIDAChip is the alignment OS for AI-native chip design — a Multiplayer AI platform that aligns intent, knowledge, and execution across all disciplines and vendors.

Three layers work as a shared nervous system for your team:
- Intent Engine (Cognitive Layer) — keeps every decision, spec, and tradeoff connected and current
- Knowledge Engine (Memory Layer) — learns from every project so the next one starts smarter
- Execution Engine (Spinal Cord) — AI teammates execute across tools, teams, and disciplines in real time

Tools optimize execution. AIDAChip coordinates the system.

The result: engineers move from alignment and coordination to intent and judgment. Tapeout cycles compress. Knowledge compounds. Smaller teams ship what large teams ship today.

Engineering, Aligned.

CONNECT
Interested in early access? Visit aidachip.com/request-access

companies.name: Every/Cora
companies.description:
Every is a media and software company founded in 2020, focusing on technology publishing and AI-powered writing tools. Led by CEO and cofounder Dan Shipper, Every provides a daily newsletter that highlights emerging technology trends and innovations in the tech industry.

The company offers an AI writing assistant designed to help users publish confidently. Additionally, Every produces a variety of educational content, including Dan Shipper's weekly column "Chain of Thought," a podcast titled "AI & I," and resources like "Dispatches From the Frontiers of AI" and "The Future of Programming." These offerings aim to inform individuals and professionals about AI applications and the evolving landscape of technology.
companies.lead_investor: Will England, Reid Hoffman, Starting Line Capital

companies.name: Keycard
companies.description:
Keycard is an identity and access management platform designed specifically for securing AI agents. It provides dynamic, task-scoped credentials that replace static secrets with real-time, cryptographically verified access controls. The company emerged from stealth in October 2025, backed by $38 million in funding from notable investors, including Andreessen Horowitz and Acrew Capital. Keycard is co-founded by industry experts with extensive experience in technology and security.

The platform offers unified identity infrastructure through software development kits (SDKs) that enable developers to create secure, identity-aware AI agent applications. Keycard's core features include dynamic access tokens for real-time verification, federated identity with distributed authorization for seamless integration, and centralized policy and auditing for governance and compliance. It aims to support developers, security teams, and enterprises in various sectors, facilitating the safe deployment of AI agents at scale.

companies.name: Riot Games
companies.description:
Riot Games, Inc. is an American video game developer and publisher based in Los Angeles, California. Founded in 2006 by Brandon Beck and Marc Merrill, the company is known for creating player-focused games, starting with its flagship title, *League of Legends*, which launched in 2009. Riot has grown significantly since its inception, expanding globally and employing over 4,500 people across more than 20 offices.

Riot develops a diverse portfolio of games set in shared universes, including *Valorant*, a tactical first-person shooter, and *Teamfight Tactics*, an auto-battler strategy game. The company also produces related media, such as the animated series *Arcane*. In addition to game development, Riot organizes esports tournaments, particularly for *League of Legends*, and is committed to community engagement and fostering a supportive ecosystem for players worldwide.

companies.name: Daily
companies.description:
Daily is an enterprise WebRTC platform founded in 2016, specializing in real-time voice, video, and AI solutions for developers. The company enables the integration of low-latency communication features into applications across various sectors, including telehealth, edtech, live commerce, event tech, creator technology, virtual collaboration, and gaming. Headquartered in San Francisco, California, Daily operates as a fully distributed remote team with approximately 126 employees.

Daily provides cross-platform SDKs and APIs that support real-time voice and video calls, as well as conversational AI agents. The platform is designed for high-quality streaming on any device or browser and includes an analytics dashboard for performance metrics and optimization. Daily has raised a total of $62.1 million in funding, with significant growth noted during the pandemic, including a 12x-30x increase in usage. The company continues to expand its team and enhance its offerings in the communication technology space.
companies.lead_investor: Sean Rose, Aston Motes, Heritage Group, David Eckstein, Moxxie Ventures, Cendana Capital, The Todd & Rahul Angel Fund, Freestyle Capital, Haystack, Tiger Global, Root Ventures, Renegade Partners, Lachy Groom, Slack Fund

companies.name: Cursor
companies.description:
Cursor is an AI-powered integrated development environment (IDE) created by Anysphere, a startup based in San Francisco. Launched in March 2023, Cursor enhances developer productivity with advanced AI features and is available for Windows, macOS, and Linux. It has quickly gained traction, achieving over $1 billion in annualized revenue by 2026 and handling significant load growth, with over 1 million queries per second.

Cursor integrates large language models to assist with coding through features like an AI code review tool called BugBot, an autocomplete function that predicts code actions, and a unique interface for agent-based workflows. It is trusted by millions of developers and is utilized by many of the largest tech companies, including Salesforce, NVIDIA, and PwC. Cursor's capabilities support enterprise teams with global configurations and accelerate software development processes.
companies.lead_investor: Thrive Capital, a16z, Accel, DST Global, Coatue, NVIDIA, Google

companies.name: Akamai
companies.description:
Akamai Technologies, Inc. is a prominent provider of cloud services, focusing on content delivery networks (CDN), cybersecurity, and cloud computing infrastructure. Founded in 1998 in Cambridge, Massachusetts, the company has developed one of the largest distributed networks globally, handling 15-30% of web traffic. Akamai's platform enhances internet performance by utilizing over 15,000 servers across more than 65 countries, ensuring reliable and high-speed delivery of web content and applications.

The company offers a range of services, including robust cybersecurity solutions, DDoS mitigation, and cloud computing infrastructure, which was expanded through the acquisition of Linode in 2022. Akamai serves a diverse clientele, including major corporations and government entities like Facebook, Twitter (now X), Bing, and NASA, providing secure and efficient experiences even during traffic surges. With a commitment to innovation and security, Akamai continues to play a vital role in the digital landscape.

companies.name: LemonSlice
companies.description:
Lemon Slice, formerly known as Infinity AI, is an AI lab focused on interactive video technology. The company specializes in creating interactive talking AI video avatars that can be generated from a single photo. Lemon Slice aims to enhance video experiences by making them interactive and responsive.

The flagship product, Lemon Slice Live, offers a real-time video chat experience powered by advanced diffusion transformer models. This technology allows users to transform any image into an engaging video character capable of real-time conversations. Lemon Slice also provides tools like Lemon Slice Studio for creating lip-synced avatars, a YouTube Intro Maker for animated intros, and API integration for embedding AI videos into applications. The platform supports over 100 AI voices and multiple languages, catering to a diverse range of creators and businesses, including those in entertainment, education, and marketing. 

Based in San Francisco, Lemon Slice is actively hiring for various positions to expand its innovative team.
companies.lead_investor: Matrix Partners

companies.name: SID AI
companies.description:
SID.ai is an AI research lab based in San Francisco, founded in 2023 and part of Y Combinator's S23 cohort. The company specializes in developing agentic retrieval models and platforms that connect large language models (LLMs) with various data sources, enhancing the accuracy and context-awareness of AI applications. Their focus on retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) helps reduce AI "hallucinations" by integrating dynamic data from sources like documents, Google Drive, Notion, email, CRMs, and Salesforce.

The core offerings include an AI-friendly API and platform that simplifies data ingestion, interpretation, storage, and querying. Key components are SID-1, an advanced retrieval model, and Capsules, which are modular data units for efficient organization. The Context Engine enables tailored data access across platforms, supporting autonomous applications with quick response times. SID.ai emphasizes privacy with self-hosted LLMs and offers a free live demo for users to test with their own data.
companies.lead_investor: Y Combinator

companies.name: Towards AI
companies.description:
Towards AI is a Pittsburgh-based company focused on building an AI community platform and providing educational resources, consulting, and development services. Founded in 2019 as a spin-off from Carnegie Mellon University, the company aims to make AI accessible to learners, professionals, and enterprises. It is led by co-founders Louie Peters and Louis-François Bouchard, who bring extensive experience in finance, AI, and software engineering.

The company offers a community platform for sharing content related to AI, machine learning, and data science, along with educational programs such as certifications and corporate bootcamps. Towards AI also provides consulting services to help organizations adopt AI technologies, including the development of custom applications and training for teams. With a strong emphasis on ethics and community engagement, Towards AI serves as a hub for over 100,000 learners and 4,000 writers, fostering collaboration and knowledge sharing in the AI field.

companies.name: Nubank
companies.description:
Nubank, founded in 2013 in São Paulo, Brazil, is a prominent digital financial services platform and neobank. It has rapidly grown to become one of the largest financial institutions in Latin America, serving 127 million customers across Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia by late 2025. The company operates as a fully digital, branchless bank, focusing on innovation, transparency, and low fees. Its mission is to simplify financial services and empower individuals through proprietary technology and data-driven products.

Nubank began with a zero-fee credit card managed via a mobile app, which quickly gained popularity. Since then, it has expanded its offerings to include digital accounts, personal loans, insurance, and investment services. The company emphasizes a customer-centric experience, providing fee-free or low-cost services and higher interest yields. Nubank targets unbanked and underbanked populations, aiming to enhance financial inclusion. With a strong presence in the market and a commitment to leveraging AI for growth, Nubank continues to redefine the banking experience for millions.

companies.name: Andon Labs
companies.description:
Andon Labs focuses on launching and scaling autonomous organizations. The company connects AI control research with practical applications, emphasizing real-world testing.

Operating under the brand VectorView.ai, Andon Labs is involved in activities related to vending operations, as indicated by their reference to Vending-Bench. This suggests a commitment to exploring innovative solutions in the vending industry within an autonomous AI framework.
companies.lead_investor: Y Combinator

companies.name: Bespoke Labs
companies.description:
Bespoke Labs is a venture-funded AI startup founded in 2024 and based in Santa Clara, California. The company specializes in data curation tools and research aimed at fine-tuning, aligning, and optimizing large language models (LLMs) and AI agents. Bespoke Labs focuses on programmability, performance, and data quality evaluation, utilizing evolutionary algorithms and post-training techniques to enhance AI development.

The company develops AI tools for data curation and post-training of LLMs, providing scalable solutions that improve AI model performance. Key products include OpenThoughts, an open reasoning dataset widely adopted by model builders, and the Bespoke-MiniChart-7B and Bespoke-MiniCheck-7B models, which excel in chart reasoning and grounded factuality, respectively. Additionally, their research on GEPA leverages evolutionary algorithms to optimize AI agents. Bespoke Labs is actively publishing research and expanding its team while operating in stealth mode.

companies.name: Sonar
companies.description:
Sonar is a software development platform that assists developers in delivering high-quality and secure code. It analyzes code from internal teams, AI-generated sources, and third-party open source libraries. The platform offers tools for static code analysis, code quality assurance, and security measures, all integrated into CI/CD workflows.

Founded in 2008 by three software engineers in France, Sonar is headquartered in Vernier, Switzerland. The company employs between 501 and 1,000 people and has gained the trust of over 400,000 organizations and 7 million developers worldwide. Its offerings support more than 30 programming languages, frameworks, and infrastructure technologies, helping developers reduce technical debt and improve their overall experience.
companies.lead_investor: Advent International, Insight Partners, Permira, General Catalyst

companies.name: Character.ai
companies.description:
Character.AI is a generative AI chatbot platform founded in November 2021 by former Google engineers Noam Shazeer and Daniel de Freitas. The platform allows users to create and interact with customizable virtual characters for various purposes, including entertainment, storytelling, learning, and social connection. Since launching its public beta in September 2022, Character.AI has gained significant traction, reaching millions of daily visitors.

The core offering is a chatbot service that enables users to engage with millions of AI-generated characters, whether real, fictional, or user-created. Key features include custom character creation, immersive conversations, and a subscription service called Character.ai+ that provides priority access and faster responses. The platform also includes tools for collaborative storytelling and community building, catering to a wide range of use cases from virtual assistance to social interaction. Character.AI aims to redefine human-AI interactions and continues to innovate in the field of conversational AI.
companies.lead_investor: Alphabet | Google

companies.name: Ref.
companies.description: Ref helps software engineering agents stay up-to-date on documentation and reduce hallucinations.

companies.name: Accenture
companies.description:
Accenture is a leading professional services and consulting firm that specializes in strategy, consulting, technology, and operations services. With a focus on digital transformation, Accenture helps enterprises reinvent their business models and enhance their digital capabilities. The company operates in over 120 countries and employs nearly 800,000 people.

Founded in the 1950s as a division of Arthur Andersen, Accenture became an independent entity in 2000 and rebranded in 2001. The firm reported revenues of $64.9 billion in 2024. Accenture's services include business strategy and transformation advisory, digital experience solutions, IT services, and business process outsourcing. The company is dedicated to assisting clients with cloud services and IT modernization, making it a trusted partner for Fortune Global 500 companies and major enterprises across various industries.

companies.name: Coder
companies.description:
Coder is a software company based in Austin, Texas, founded in 2017. It specializes in self-hosted cloud development environments (CDEs) that facilitate secure and scalable remote development on customer infrastructure. Coder aims to enhance developer productivity by simplifying the configuration, security, and management of development environments. The company operates on a remote-first model and employs around 100-1000 people across various countries.

The core product offered by Coder is a self-hosted CDE that can be deployed on cloud or on-premises infrastructure. It supports any git repository, integrated development environment (IDE), and developer environment, ensuring consistent and efficient remote workspaces. Coder provides a Community Edition that is free and open-source, as well as a Premium/Enterprise version that includes additional features such as ticket-based support, multi-tenancy, and advanced resource management. Coder's technology stack includes popular tools and frameworks like Golang, Kubernetes, and Docker, enabling rapid workspace provisioning and collaboration between developers and AI agents.
companies.lead_investor: KKR, Uncork Capital, Qube Research & Technologies

companies.name: ServiceNow
companies.description:
ServiceNow is a prominent provider of enterprise cloud computing solutions, established in 2004 by Fred Luddy. The company offers a SaaS platform that automates and manages business workflows across various sectors, including IT, HR, customer service, and security operations. Initially focused on IT service management, ServiceNow has evolved into a comprehensive low-code workflow automation platform, enabling organizations to build and deploy applications with minimal coding.

The core of ServiceNow's offerings is the Now Platform, which supports rapid adaptation to business needs and enhances operational efficiency. The platform includes features for IT service management, HR service delivery, customer service management, and security operations. With AI-driven automation and integrations, ServiceNow streamlines processes for large organizations, reducing complexity and costs compared to traditional on-premise solutions. The company emphasizes a customer-first culture and has achieved significant growth, holding Fortune 500 status with a market cap exceeding $114 billion.

companies.name: Gates Foundation
companies.description:
The Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation, founded in 2000 by Bill Gates and Melinda French Gates, is the largest private philanthropic organization in the world. Headquartered in Seattle, Washington, it has assets of $77.2 billion and an annual giving of approximately $8 billion. The foundation's mission is to create a world where everyone has the opportunity to live a healthy and productive life by addressing inequities in health, poverty, education, and development.

The foundation focuses on three main program areas: Global Health, Global Development, and the United States Program. It targets infectious diseases, maternal and child health, and nutrition, while also addressing issues like education and access to technology in low-income communities. The foundation emphasizes scalable, evidence-based solutions and partnerships, providing grants to organizations and research institutions to support high-impact projects in over 100 countries. Its work includes initiatives like the Gates Cambridge Scholarships and the Grand Challenges in Global Health, aimed at improving health outcomes in developing regions.

companies.name: Form3
companies.description:
Form3 is a cloud-native payment technology platform provider based in London, UK. Founded in 2016, the company specializes in enterprise-grade payment processing solutions for financial institutions. Form3 processes over £4 billion in annual transaction volume and serves major banks such as Mastercard, Nationwide, Lloyds, and Barclays.

The core offering is a fully managed Payments-as-a-Service platform built on a microservices architecture. This platform features a multi-cloud setup that ensures zero-downtime payment processing and real-time transaction handling. Financial institutions benefit from a single API integration that simplifies access to multiple payment gateways and services. Form3 also provides comprehensive payment services, including payment orchestration, fraud prevention, and regulatory compliance management, allowing clients to focus on their core business. The platform is designed for scalability and resilience, making it suitable for both large enterprise banks and digital challenger banks across the US, UK, and EU markets.
companies.lead_investor: Nationwide

companies.name: DoorDash
companies.description:
DoorDash is a global on-demand delivery platform founded in 2013 that connects consumers with local businesses, primarily for food delivery. The company operates in over 40 countries, utilizing technology and logistics through a network of independent contractors known as Dashers. DoorDash began as PaloAltoDelivery.com, created by Stanford students to address delivery gaps for small businesses. It has since grown to serve millions of users and holds a significant market share in the U.S. online food delivery sector.

The core offering of DoorDash includes on-demand delivery of food and other items from local restaurants and businesses. Key services include DashPass, a subscription for unlimited deliveries, DoorDash Drive, which provides delivery infrastructure for businesses, and DoorDash Storefront, which helps businesses create online ordering systems. The platform has expanded its focus from just restaurant delivery to broader local commerce, emphasizing technology to enhance efficiency and access for both consumers and merchants.

companies.name: Gradium
companies.description:
Gradium is an AI company that specializes in developing advanced audio language models for natural and expressive voice interactions. Their technology enables ultra-low latency voice processing, making it suitable for a variety of applications, including voice chats and interactive experiences.

The company offers several key products, including Text-to-Speech (TTS) for converting text into realistic speech, and Speech-to-Text (STT) for accurate real-time transcription. Gradium's voice chat and interactive models support dynamic voice modulation and multimodal features, such as generating images during conversations. Their core audio language models are designed for scalability and high performance, facilitating seamless user experiences across different platforms.
companies.lead_investor: Rodolphe Saadé, Xavier Niel, FirstMark, Amplify Partners, Eurazeo, Eric Schmidt, DST Global, Korelya Capital

companies.name: Jellyfish
companies.description:
Jellyfish is a Boston-based SaaS company founded in 2017, specializing in Software Engineering Intelligence. Their platform helps engineering, finance, and business leaders optimize research and development organizations by providing actionable insights on team health, performance, investment allocations, and delivery. The platform utilizes a patented Unified Model to connect software development lifecycle data to business outcomes, enhancing visibility without requiring manual cleanup.

The Engineering Management Platform integrates with tools like Jira, GitHub, and CI/CD systems to deliver data-driven insights. Key features include business alignment for resource prioritization, identification of inefficiencies, predictive delivery management, and monitoring of team health. Jellyfish also offers customizable dashboards and guidance for planning and organizational alignment, supporting engineering teams from planning to delivery.

Over 500 organizations, including Clari, Hootsuite, and Priceline, trust Jellyfish for its insights into portfolios and resource allocation. The company has a workforce of 201-500 employees and continues to expand following a $12M Series A funding round in 2020.
companies.lead_investor: Insight Partners, Accel, Tiger Global, Wing Venture Capital

companies.name: Surge AI
companies.description:
Surge AI is a San Francisco-based company founded in 2020 that specializes in data labeling for training artificial intelligence models, particularly large language models (LLMs). The company provides infrastructure for AI development through high-quality data annotation and feedback mechanisms, which help create AI outputs that are more helpful and aligned with human needs. Surge AI operates from its headquarters in San Francisco, with additional offices in New York and San Diego.

The core platform of Surge AI focuses on data labeling and AI training services. This includes supervised fine-tuning of models using annotated datasets and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) to enhance LLMs. The company employs expert human annotators and offers 24/7 support to ensure secure and private data for building and improving AI models across various industries, including travel, tourism, manufacturing, and space. Surge AI collaborates with major enterprises and institutions, such as Microsoft and Nvidia, to support high-stakes AI projects.

companies.name: Fleet
companies.description: Gym Environments for Frontier Agents
companies.lead_investor: SV Angel, Sequoia, Bain Capital Ventures, Menlo Ventures

companies.name: Salesforce
companies.description:
Salesforce is a leading American technology company founded in 1999, known for pioneering cloud-based customer relationship management (CRM) software. Headquartered in San Francisco, California, Salesforce operates globally with offices in various countries, including Australia, India, Germany, the UK, Japan, France, Ireland, and Spain. The company serves businesses of all sizes, helping them achieve digital transformation through automation and customer-focused platforms.

Salesforce offers a comprehensive AI-powered CRM platform that is accessible from any device. Key components include Sales Cloud for automating sales processes, Service Cloud for customer support automation, and Marketing Cloud for managing marketing efforts. The company also provides various platform tools, such as Apex for data processing, Visualforce for custom user interfaces, and Salesforce Lightning for enhanced UI experiences. Additionally, Salesforce has developed mobile applications and a vibrant ecosystem through its AppExchange marketplace, which features a wide range of applications.

companies.name: MiniMax
companies.description:
MiniMax AI is a leading artificial intelligence technology company founded in early 2022. The company focuses on advancing AI through self-developed multimodal large models, including MiniMax M2, Hailuo 2.3, Speech 2.6, and Music 2.0. These models excel in various capabilities, such as code processing, ultra-long context handling, and managing text, audio, images, video, and music. MiniMax serves over 212 million individual users and more than 100,000 enterprise customers and developers worldwide.

The company offers a range of AI-native products and APIs designed for multimodal generation and real-time interactions. Key offerings include MiniMax Agent for creative tasks, Hailuo AI for video creation, and advanced audio and speech models that provide hyper-realistic text-to-speech with emotional intelligence. MiniMax also features a Realtime API for low-latency multimodal processing and an open platform for developers to create innovative applications. These products aim to enhance productivity and deliver immersive experiences across various sectors.

companies.name: Monday
companies.description:
monday.com is an Israeli-based cloud platform that serves as a Work OS, enabling users to create custom applications, workflows, and project management tools. Founded in 2012 as DaPulse and rebranded in 2017, the company originated as an internal tool at Wix.com to improve communication and transparency. It launched its product commercially in 2014 and has since grown significantly, achieving unicorn status with a valuation of $1.9 billion in 2019 and going public on Nasdaq in 2021.

The core offering of monday.com is a flexible, low-code Work OS that allows users to build custom work management applications, automate workflows, and integrate data without extensive coding knowledge. Key features include the monday 2.0 platform, which supports automation, integrations, and a marketplace for third-party apps. With over 127,000 customers across more than 200 business verticals, monday.com caters to a wide range of industries, emphasizing self-serve adoption and organic growth. The company is headquartered in Tel Aviv, Israel, with additional offices in major cities worldwide.

companies.name: MercadoLibre
companies.description:
Mercado Libre is a prominent e-commerce and fintech company based in Latin America, founded in 1999 in Buenos Aires, Argentina. It was established by Marcos Galperin and his co-founders, inspired by the eBay model. Initially launched as an online auction marketplace for pre-owned goods, Mercado Libre has grown into a comprehensive platform that supports commerce, payments, logistics, and services across 18 countries. The company employs over 11,000 people and has been listed on NASDAQ since 2007.

The core offerings of Mercado Libre include its marketplace for buying and selling new and used goods, the Mercado Pago fintech payment solution, and Mercado Envíos, a logistics service for efficient delivery. The platform serves a significant number of small and medium enterprises, with 73% of its sellers being family businesses, and it supports 1.8 million families who rely on it for their primary income. Mercado Libre emphasizes technological innovation and socio-economic impact, aiming to empower consumers and businesses in underserved markets throughout the region.

companies.name: UC Berkeley
companies.description:
The University of California, Berkeley (UC Berkeley) is a prestigious public research university established in 1868. It is the oldest campus in the University of California system and is located on a 1,232-acre campus in Berkeley, California, approximately 15 miles from San Francisco. UC Berkeley is known for its academic excellence, innovative teaching, and commitment to public service, emphasizing interdisciplinary education and addressing significant social and global challenges.

UC Berkeley offers a wide range of undergraduate and graduate programs, including multidisciplinary studies, humanities, social sciences, business through the Haas School, entrepreneurship, technology, and engineering. The university supports a mission of teaching, research, and public service, contributing to California's economy and society. With a rich history of research and discovery, UC Berkeley has produced numerous faculty awards and notable alumni who have made significant impacts across various industries. The campus also boasts a vibrant community with unique traditions and a commitment to fostering creativity and intellectual growth.

companies.name: Edge And Node
companies.description:
Edge & Node is a leading provider of blockchain data infrastructure, focusing on scalable indexing and secure access for enterprise applications. The company supports various use cases, including real-world asset tokenization and stablecoins, through its innovative tools and protocols. Their core product, The Graph, is a data marketplace that standardizes Subgraphs for efficient indexing and querying of blockchain data using GraphQL APIs. 

The company also offers the Graph Node, which enables transparent blockchain systems by providing secure access to contract states and audit trails. Additionally, Edge & Node has developed the x402 Management Platform for agent payments and operations, along with solutions for tokenized assets and stablecoins that ensure data quality and compliance. 

Edge & Node operates The House, a collaborative space for Web3 and AI innovators, hosting events, workshops, and co-creation opportunities. They emphasize security and compliance in their services, providing tailored solutions to meet the needs of enterprise clients.

companies.name: Atlan
companies.description:
Atlan is an active metadata platform designed for modern data teams. It helps users discover, understand, trust, and collaborate on data assets across various environments, including cloud data lakes and warehouses. The platform serves as a collaborative workspace and metadata control plane, automating cataloging, governance, lineage tracking, and documentation. 

Founded in 2019 and headquartered in Singapore, Atlan launched its first version in 2020 and has since raised $16 million in Series A funding. The company emphasizes AI-driven solutions and open metadata infrastructure, managing over $10 trillion in enterprise value for its customers. Atlan offers features such as data discovery, governance, collaboration tools, and extensive integrations with over 140 data sources. It provides various pricing tiers to accommodate different needs, from basic cataloging to advanced governance solutions.
companies.lead_investor: Salesforce Ventures, Meritech Capital, GIC, Peak XV Partners

companies.name: DFPI
companies.description:
The State of California is a U.S. state government that operates under a constitution with three branches: executive, legislative, and judicial. It serves approximately 39 million residents by enacting, enforcing, and interpreting laws. The executive branch is led by the Governor, who oversees law enforcement, budget proposals, and appointments. The legislative branch consists of a bicameral Legislature that drafts laws and allocates taxes, while the judicial branch interprets laws and resolves disputes through the Supreme Court and lower courts.

California's government provides essential public services across various sectors, including lawmaking, public safety, education, health, and infrastructure. The state ensures fair elections and oversees local governance through counties and cities. The government is dedicated to serving the needs of its residents and maintaining the state's public administration.
companies.lead_investor: PG&E

companies.name: Auditoria AI
companies.description:
Auditoria.AI is an AI-enabled finance automation platform designed to enhance accounting and finance operations. The company utilizes artificial intelligence, machine learning, and robotic process automation to streamline key finance processes, improve compliance, and increase business visibility. By automating routine finance tasks, Auditoria allows finance teams to concentrate on more strategic business functions.

The platform offers several AI-powered solutions, including SmartCollections for accounts receivable automation, SmartVendor for accounts payable and vendor management, and SmartFlow Skills for advanced analytics. These solutions help organizations manage collections, streamline procurement workflows, and generate cash flow forecasts efficiently. Auditoria serves a diverse range of customers, including CFOs, finance teams, shared services, and IT departments, providing them with tools to modernize finance processes and improve operational efficiency.
companies.lead_investor: Engineering Capital, KPMG Ventures, Sentinel Global, Venrock, Neotribe, Dell Technologies Capital, Innovius Capital

companies.name: WSO2
companies.description:
WSO2 is an open-source technology company founded in 2005, specializing in software and cloud solutions for application development, API integration, identity and access management, and digital transformation. The company enables enterprises to manage over 60 trillion transactions and 1 billion identities each year. WSO2 has evolved its offerings from initial products to a comprehensive API-first platform with over 24 interconnected middleware components.

Headquartered in Sri Lanka, WSO2 has expanded globally with offices in multiple countries, including Australia, Brazil, Germany, India, the UAE, the UK, and the US. The company is recognized for its financial strength and high subscription growth, serving thousands of enterprises, including many Fortune 500 companies. WSO2's open-source software is designed to support hybrid environments, allowing organizations to avoid vendor lock-in while facilitating API-led digital transformation.
companies.lead_investor: EQT Group

companies.name: Hinge Health
companies.description:
Hinge Health is a digital health company founded in 2014, focusing on virtual musculoskeletal (MSK) care. The company aims to improve accessibility and reduce chronic pain, unnecessary surgeries, and reliance on opioids through technology and clinical expertise. Hinge Health operates a patient-centered Digital MSK Clinic that utilizes AI-powered motion tracking, wearable devices, and a clinical team of physical therapists, physicians, and health coaches.

The company offers end-to-end virtual physical therapy through its app, featuring personalized exercise plans, 24/7 therapist access, and mental health support. Key products include Hinge Connect, which integrates electronic medical records to monitor patient activity, and Enso, a wearable device for pain relief. Hinge Health serves a diverse clientele, including self-insured employers and health plans, reaching millions of members and a significant number of Fortune 500 companies. The platform emphasizes automated care delivery and aims to enhance health equity through remote access.

companies.name: Theta Software
companies.description:
Theta Software is an AI company focused on developing infrastructure for self-learning AI agents. Their core product is an intelligent memory layer that allows agents to adapt in real-time and learn from past interactions. This technology helps improve the performance of AI agents in complex, real-world workflows. By adding just a few lines of code, developers can integrate this memory layer into existing systems, enhancing accuracy and efficiency.

The company also offers simulation environments that replicate real-world workspaces, enabling the training of expert-level agents. These self-learning agents continuously improve their performance through iterative learning. Founded by experienced professionals from the AI and machine learning sectors, Theta Software is backed by Y Combinator and aims to support developers in creating reliable AI solutions for dynamic applications.

companies.name: .txt
companies.description:
.txt is a company that specializes in structured generation libraries for developers and AI teams. Their mission is to enable reliable LLM-driven applications by providing schema-compliant outputs that align with JSON schemas, regular expressions, or grammars. Founded to improve the efficiency of parsing and validating LLM outputs, .txt focuses on creating tools for mission-critical APIs and scaling AI deployment across organizations.

The company offers high-performance libraries under commercial licenses, including dotjson, dotregex, and dotgrammar. These libraries help constrain LLM outputs to predefined formats, ensuring accuracy and predictability. Their technology supports hosted solutions on AWS Marketplace and emphasizes rapid scaling of large language models. .txt also contributes to open-source projects like Outlines and Outlines-core, which facilitate structured text generation without additional inference costs. The team is dedicated to enhancing prompt efficiency and generating clean, analyzable outputs for various applications.

companies.name: Tailscale
companies.description:
Tailscale Inc. is a software company based in Toronto, Ontario, founded in 2019 by former Google engineers. The company specializes in a software-defined mesh VPN service called Tailscale, which enables secure, zero-config peer-to-peer networking. Launched in 2020, Tailscale quickly gained popularity, particularly during the rise of remote work, and has since attracted a diverse customer base, including small businesses and Fortune 500 companies.

Tailscale's VPN-as-a-service is built on WireGuard and offers features like peer-to-peer connectivity, web-based management, and support for multiple platforms, including iOS, macOS, Windows, and Linux. The service simplifies networking for developers and IT teams, enhancing productivity for remote and hybrid work environments. Tailscale emphasizes ease of use and aims to rebuild the internet around secure connections, making it a valuable tool for organizations needing efficient and secure networking solutions.
companies.lead_investor: Accel, Insight Partners, CRV, Uncork Capital, Anthony Casalena, George Kurtz, Heavybit

companies.name: You.com / Recursive Superintelligence
companies.description:
You.com is an enterprise AI productivity engine and search infrastructure platform founded in 2020 by Richard Socher and Bryan McCann, former Salesforce AI researchers. Originally launched as a privacy-focused consumer search engine, it has evolved into a B2B provider of AI-powered search, APIs, and tools, processing over 1 billion queries monthly.

The company offers a range of AI-driven tools designed for enterprise use, including a core search engine that understands queries and summarizes web content, a conversational AI assistant called YouChat, an AI writing tool named YouWrite, and an image generation tool called YouImagine. You.com also provides an enterprise API and infrastructure that supports high-volume queries and integrates seamlessly into business workflows. With a focus on data transparency and customization, You.com serves thousands of enterprise customers across various sectors, including finance, e-commerce, and media.
companies.lead_investor: Norwest Venture Partners, Salesforce Ventures, Georgian, Cox Enterprises

companies.name: Twilio
companies.description:
Twilio Inc. is a cloud communications company based in San Francisco, California, founded in 2008. It provides programmable APIs that enable developers to create customer engagement experiences through voice calls, text messaging, email, video, and more. Twilio launched its first product, Twilio Voice, in 2008, followed by its SMS API in 2010. The company went public in 2016 and has since expanded globally, operating 25 offices in 17 countries.

Twilio's Customer Engagement Platform offers a range of cloud-based tools for programmable communications. Key services include APIs for voice, messaging (SMS, MMS, WhatsApp), email through SendGrid, video, and authentication solutions. The platform supports high-volume traffic and is designed to help developers build digital experiences across various communication channels. Twilio has also grown through strategic acquisitions, enhancing its capabilities and reach in the communications space.

companies.name: JP Morgan Chase
companies.description:
JPMorgan Chase & Co. is one of the largest financial institutions globally, formed in 2000 from the merger of J.P. Morgan & Co. and Chase Manhattan Bank. The company has a rich history that dates back to 1799, with numerous mergers and acquisitions, including significant entities like Bank One and Bear Stearns. It has evolved into a leader in financial services, with a strong emphasis on innovation in commercial, investment, and private banking.

The firm offers a wide range of financial services, including commercial and investment banking, retail and consumer banking, and asset management. JPMorgan Chase has played a pivotal role in financing major U.S. projects and supporting economic growth throughout its history. With its headquarters at iconic locations like 23 Wall Street, the company continues to be a key player in the global financial landscape.

companies.name: OpenHands
companies.description:
OpenHands is an open-source platform that focuses on secure and transparent cloud coding agents for autonomous software development. It enables users to create and manage AI-driven development agents, which can be run locally or scaled in the cloud. The platform emphasizes security, transparency, and composability, featuring a lightweight SDK and a streamlined command-line interface.

The core offering is the Software Agent SDK, a Python library that supports agentic technology and large language model orchestration. OpenHands provides various tools, including the OpenHands CLI for quick coding tasks, a Local GUI for running agents on personal devices, and a Cloud version with integrations for popular platforms like GitHub and Slack. For larger enterprises, OpenHands offers a self-hosted solution with extended support options. The platform supports multiple runtimes and is compatible with various large language models, allowing for flexible deployment and configuration.
companies.lead_investor: Menlo Ventures, Obvious Ventures, Madrona Venture Group, Alumni Ventures, Fujitsu Ventures Fund

companies.name: Navan
companies.description:
Navan, Inc., formerly known as TripActions, is a corporate travel and expense management company based in Palo Alto, California. Founded in May 2015 by Ariel Cohen and Ilan Twig, Navan offers an all-in-one platform that supports travel programs for over 10,000 companies globally. The platform integrates travel booking, expense management, payments, reporting, and analytics to enhance business travel experiences.

Navan provides a comprehensive travel management solution that includes online booking for flights, hotels, and events, along with 24/7 support through its AI agent, Ava. The company emphasizes user-friendly design and offers features like custom policies, real-time insights, and Navan Rewards for budget-compliant bookings. Its expense management services include photo-based receipt submission and rapid reimbursements across multiple currencies. Navan also focuses on analytics, reporting, and employee safety, ensuring a modern and efficient approach to corporate travel.

companies.name: Deno
companies.description:
Deno is an open-source runtime for JavaScript, TypeScript, and WebAssembly, built on the V8 engine and Rust. It offers a modern alternative to Node.js, featuring a permission-based security model, integrated package management, and built-in tooling. Co-created by Ryan Dahl, the original creator of Node.js, Deno aims to simplify web development while ensuring security and compatibility with web standards.

The Deno ecosystem includes several key components. The Deno Runtime allows for executing code with full web API compatibility and enterprise-grade support. Deno Deploy is a serverless platform for hosting applications with low latency, supporting various frameworks and offering usage-based pricing. Deno Subhosting provides a secure environment for SaaS providers to manage and scale customer code. Additional tools include the Fresh web framework, cloud-native solutions, and consulting services for enterprises. Deno monetizes through managed cloud services while keeping the core runtime free to encourage widespread adoption.
companies.lead_investor: Long Journey Ventures, Shasta Ventures, Mozilla, Sequoia Consulting Group, Sequoia, Four Rivers Group, Rauch Capital

companies.name: Fidelity Investments
companies.description:
Fidelity Investments is a leading financial services company based in Boston, Massachusetts, founded in 1946 by Edward C. Johnson II. With approximately $9.9 trillion in customer assets and around 77,000 employees, Fidelity is one of the largest asset managers in the world. The company has a rich history, evolving from a small advisory firm into a global leader in investment solutions.

Fidelity offers a wide range of financial products and services, including mutual funds, retirement solutions, brokerage services, and innovative investment options. Their mutual fund offerings include growth-oriented funds, sector-specific funds, and zero-expense-ratio funds. Fidelity also provides 401(k) plans, pension management, and donor-advised funds. Additionally, they offer discount and online brokerage services, cryptocurrency custody and trading, and wealth management. Fidelity serves over 40 million individual investors and numerous institutional clients, making it a key player in the financial services industry.

companies.name: TikTok
companies.description:
TikTok is a short-form video sharing and social media platform owned by ByteDance Ltd. It allows users worldwide to create, discover, and share entertaining content. TikTok operates independently from mainland China, with headquarters in Los Angeles and Singapore. The platform fosters creativity and community, supporting economic growth for creators and businesses through features like shoppable videos, LIVE streams, and personalized feeds.

A key offering is TikTok Shop, an integrated e-commerce platform that enables in-app purchases, allowing users to shop seamlessly while engaging with content. Since its launch in 2021, TikTok Shop has rapidly grown, becoming a significant player in online retail. The platform also provides advertising solutions, including real-time campaign tools and targeted ads, helping brands connect with engaged audiences. TikTok serves as a valuable resource for small businesses and global brands across various sectors, including food, beauty, and fashion.

companies.name: Pydantic
companies.description:
Pydantic is a developer tools company focused on Python, founded by Samuel Colvin. The company has transitioned from an open-source project to an enterprise solution, supported by Sequoia Capital, Partech, and Irregular Expressions. Pydantic aims to create the best developer experience, emphasizing transparency, collaboration, and excellence.

The company offers a platform with three main components: Pydantic Validation, the leading data validation library for Python; Pydantic AI, an agent framework and LLM library that enhances AI development; and Pydantic Logfire, an AI observability platform that monitors traces, logs, and metrics. Pydantic is trusted by millions of developers and major organizations, including Datadog, which utilizes Pydantic for configuration validation, and various AI research teams that report significant improvements in their workflows. Pydantic operates as a remote-first company, fostering a culture that empowers developers to lead and innovate.
companies.lead_investor: Irregular Expressions, Partech, Sequoia

companies.name: Composo
companies.description:
Composo is a London-based AI startup founded by CEO Seb Fox and CTO Luke Markham. The company focuses on developing a testing and optimization platform for large language model (LLM) applications. Composo addresses challenges in LLM app development, such as prompt crafting, model selection, and performance monitoring. Its platform is designed for developers, product managers, and enterprises, providing tools for rapid iteration and performance testing.

The core platform offers deterministic scoring, enabling accurate evaluations without the need for complex rubrics. Key features include real-time monitoring, instant scoring on changes, and an experimentation interface for non-technical users. Composo integrates seamlessly into CI/CD pipelines and supports various applications, including customer service automation, medical triage chatbots, and legal contract generation. The company has recently secured funding to enhance its engineering capabilities and advance research and development in agentic AI.
companies.lead_investor: Leon Szeli, Tomislav Tomov, EWOR, Twin Path Ventures, JVH Ventures

companies.name: Two Sigma Investments
companies.description:
Two Sigma is a technology-driven investment management firm founded in 2001, specializing in quantitative strategies that leverage data science, algorithms, and advanced computing. Headquartered in New York City, the firm has a global presence with offices in Houston, Portland, London, Tokyo, Hong Kong, and Shanghai. It employs over 1,800 people, including a significant number with advanced degrees and PhDs, fostering a culture of scientific research and engineering.

The firm focuses on algorithmic trading across various liquid markets, including equities, futures, and foreign exchange. Two Sigma also engages in private investments and venture capital through Two Sigma Ventures, targeting early-stage companies in data science and technology. The company emphasizes innovation and infrastructure, utilizing extensive computing resources to support its investment strategies. It primarily serves institutional clients, including pensions and sovereign wealth funds, and is recognized for its high returns and innovative approach to investment management.

companies.name: turbopuffer
companies.description:
turbopuffer is a serverless vector and full-text search database designed for fast and scalable search capabilities, particularly for AI applications and large data retrieval tasks. Founded by Simon Hørup Eskildsen and Justine Li, the company aims to make every byte searchable by connecting AI with extensive, fresh data. Based in Canada with remote operations and offices in Ottawa, Vancouver, and New York City, turbopuffer emphasizes cost efficiency and high performance.

The unified search platform combines vector and full-text search features, offering automatic scaling, low latency, and support for billions of vectors. It can handle significant production workloads, including over 1 trillion documents and high query throughput. Ideal use cases include AI applications, semantic search, recommendation systems, and large-scale document retrieval. The platform is available by referral or invitation.
companies.lead_investor: Lux Capital, Thrive Capital, Lachy Groom

companies.name: Sakana.ai
companies.description:
Sakana AI is a Tokyo-based artificial intelligence research and development company founded in 2023. The company specializes in nature-inspired AI technologies, focusing on evolutionary algorithms and collective intelligence to create foundation models tailored for Japan. Sakana AI aims to address national challenges such as a declining workforce and an aging population through innovative and sustainable AI solutions.

The team includes notable experts from leading tech companies, and Sakana AI has attracted global talent to Tokyo. The company has secured significant funding, including a $30 million seed round and a $200 million Series A round, positioning it as a key player in Japan's AI landscape. Sakana AI develops customized AI products and collaborates with various Japanese institutions, including major financial organizations like MUFG and Daiwa Securities Group, to create models that reflect Japan's unique language, culture, and industry needs.
companies.lead_investor: Mitsubishi Electric Corporation

companies.name: Hamming AI
companies.description:
Hamming AI is a startup based in Austin, Texas, founded in 2024 and backed by Y Combinator. The company specializes in automated testing, monitoring, and optimization for AI voice agents, ensuring reliability across various industries. Hamming AI's platform allows for rapid and cost-effective testing by simulating thousands of phone calls, enabling 20 times faster and 10 times cheaper testing compared to traditional methods.

The company offers a comprehensive voice and chat agent QA platform that automates scenario generation, call replay, and metric tracking. Key features include real-time analytics, automated vulnerability detection, and load testing. Hamming AI supports high-stakes applications such as appointment scheduling, customer support, and clinical trials, catering primarily to B2B teams in the technology sector. The company has raised $4.3 million in seed funding and is recognized as a Challenger in the voice AI development market.
companies.lead_investor: Hiten Shah, Mischief, Ran Makavy, Olive Tree Capital, Kulveer Taggar, Y Combinator, Transpose Platform, Coalition Operators, TwentyTwo VC, Richard Aberman, Orange Collective, Coughdrop Capital, Pioneer Fund, AI Grant, Max Kolysh

companies.name: Ironclad
companies.description:
Ironclad is a San Francisco-based SaaS company founded in 2014 that specializes in AI-powered contract lifecycle management (CLM) software. The platform is designed to assist legal, sales, finance, and procurement teams in efficiently creating, negotiating, signing, storing, and analyzing contracts. Ironclad's cloud-based solution offers customizable workflows, Docx-native editing, and advanced AI features, including an AI assistant named Jurist that helps with drafting, editing, and summarizing contracts.

The company has achieved significant milestones, including multiple funding rounds and the acquisition of PactSafe. Ironclad's platform emphasizes faster deal-making, high user adoption rates, and cost reductions. It integrates seamlessly with existing technology stacks and provides robust security features, making it a preferred choice for enterprise clients, particularly in financial services and crypto. The software enhances collaboration and offers insights through centralized approvals, risk tracking, and analytics, ultimately streamlining the entire contract management process.
companies.lead_investor: Lux Capital, Franklin Resources, Franklin Templeton Investments, Sequoia, Haystack, Accel, Y Combinator, Bond, Emergence

companies.name: Morph
companies.description: specialized inference optimization for codegen

companies.name: IBM
companies.description:
IBM, or International Business Machines Corporation, is a leading multinational technology and consulting company based in Armonk, New York. Founded in 1911, IBM has a rich history that began with the merger of three data-processing firms. The company has played a significant role in the evolution of computing, particularly with its entry into the mainframe computer market in the 1950s, where it quickly became a dominant force.

IBM's diverse product portfolio includes mainframe computers, personal computers, supercomputers, software, and semiconductor chips. The company is known for its innovative contributions, such as the development of the first widely accepted high-level programming language, FORTRAN. With a strong focus on research and development, IBM holds over 40,000 active patents, showcasing its commitment to innovation and technology advancement.

companies.name: t3.gg
companies.description:
Theo AI is a legal software company based in Palo Alto, California, founded in 2024. The company specializes in an AI-driven predictive platform designed to forecast outcomes of legal disputes by utilizing historical case data, real-time analytics, and proprietary machine learning models. The founding team includes experts from law, technology, and finance, with backgrounds from institutions like Stanford University and the University of Chicago.

The core product of Theo AI is a predictive engine that estimates case success rates, recovery ranges, and financial outcomes for litigation disputes. Users can input case factors and receive an initial quality score for prediction accuracy. The platform analyzes similar historical cases to provide insights on win probabilities, award estimates, and key arguments. It also offers real-time insights and expanded analytics to support legal professionals in making informed decisions. Theo AI aims to enhance clarity in the legal industry by providing actionable, data-driven predictions for legal professionals and firms.

companies.name: Not Diamond
companies.description:
Not Diamond, Inc. is an AI optimization platform based in San Francisco, with additional offices in New York and support for remote work. The company focuses on providing intelligent model routing and automatic prompt adaptation, enabling enterprises and developers to select and optimize AI models for improved accuracy, efficiency, and security. Not Diamond aims to create a robust infrastructure for a multi-model future, similar to Kubernetes for cloud-native applications.

The platform features dynamic model routing, which selects from thousands of foundation models and custom engines based on various performance metrics. It also includes prompt adaptation that generates and tests numerous prompt variations to enhance results while reducing manual engineering time. Not Diamond serves Fortune 100 companies and leading startups, including SAP, which utilizes the platform for engineering efficiency. The company is backed by notable figures in the AI industry and maintains SOC-2 compliance, ensuring secure client-side execution and data handling.
companies.lead_investor: IBM, Deepwater, Sap.io, Defy Partners, Ambush Capital, Myriad Venture Partners, DNX

companies.name: Theory Ventures
companies.description:
Theory Ventures is an early-stage venture capital firm located in San Francisco, CA, founded in 2023. The firm focuses on investing in software companies that utilize technology discontinuities to gain market advantages. With a thesis-driven investment approach, Theory Ventures targets Seed and Series A stages, investing between $1 million and $25 million in each company.

The firm manages a total of $688 million in assets and has launched two funds: Theory Ventures Fund I and Theory Ventures Fund II, which closed with $450 million in November 2024. Theory Ventures aims to build a concentrated portfolio, planning to hold 12-15 core positions in each fund. Founded by Tomasz Tunguz, a recognized figure in the industry, the firm is supported by a small team, including Chief Financial Officer Kristin Beach.

companies.name: Intuit
companies.description:
Intuit is a leading American financial software company founded in 1983 by Scott Cook and Tom Proulx. Headquartered in Mountain View, California, Intuit specializes in user-friendly tools for personal finance, small business accounting, and tax preparation. The company has grown significantly, serving over 50 million customers worldwide with a workforce of more than 8,000 employees.

Intuit's product lineup includes Quicken for personal finance management, QuickBooks for small business accounting, TurboTax for tax preparation, and Mint for personal finance tracking. The company focuses on simplicity and accessibility, ensuring that its software is easy to use for individuals and small businesses alike. Intuit emphasizes customer-centric design and innovation, continually testing products with real users to enhance their offerings.

companies.name: Boundary
companies.description:
Boundary is an AI development toolkit company based in Seattle, Washington, founded in 2023 by Vaibhav Gupta and Aaron Villalpando Gonzalez. The company, formerly known as Gloo, specializes in creating developer tools for structured generation using large language models (LLMs). Boundary participated in Y Combinator's Winter 2023 batch and has secured $500K in funding from various investors.

The flagship product of Boundary is BAML, an open-source domain-specific language that assists developers in building, testing, and managing prompts with structured outputs. BAML allows developers to write prompts as functions with defined input and output types, enhancing the process of prompt engineering. Key features include type-safe responses from LLMs, compatibility with major LLM providers, automatic retry mechanisms, testing capabilities, and code generation in Python and TypeScript. Boundary aims to help developers create reliable AI systems by minimizing errors and ensuring consistent performance.
companies.lead_investor: Y Combinator

companies.name: AWS
companies.description:
Amazon is a leading e-commerce and cloud computing company based in Seattle, Washington. Founded by Jeff Bezos in 1994, it began as an online bookstore and has grown to become the world's largest internet-based retailer. The company focuses on simplifying online transactions and enhancing customer experiences.

Amazon offers a wide range of products and services, including online retail across various categories such as electronics, groceries, and toys. It provides third-party seller services and operates Amazon Web Services (AWS), a major cloud computing platform. The company also features digital products like the Kindle e-reader, Alexa smart speakers, and Prime Video streaming service. Additionally, Amazon Prime offers subscription services for fast delivery and exclusive content.

With a customer base of hundreds of millions across more than 20 countries, Amazon is dedicated to improving customer satisfaction through competitive pricing, extensive product selection, and efficient delivery options. The company continues to expand its offerings and innovate in the retail and technology sectors.

companies.name: Arcee AI
companies.description:
Arcee AI is a Miami-based open-intelligence lab and AI startup founded by former Hugging Face engineers. The company specializes in developing and deploying efficient, secure small language models (SLMs) and open-weight frontier models for enterprises, particularly in regulated industries. Launched in February 2025, Arcee AI raised $24 million in Series A funding to enhance its offerings, focusing on secure generative AI solutions that operate within customers' virtual private clouds to ensure data privacy.

Arcee AI provides an end-to-end platform for building, training, fine-tuning, merging, deploying, and monitoring SLMs and custom generative AI models. Key features include model merging, optimized training processes, and tools for creating custom workflows. The platform is designed to support scalability on AWS infrastructure, making it suitable for organizations with proprietary data. Arcee AI targets highly regulated sectors such as legal, healthcare, insurance, and financial services, enabling secure generative AI applications that deliver high returns on investment.
companies.lead_investor: Long Journey Ventures, Flybridge Capital Partners, Emergence, Centre Street Partners

companies.name: Onlay
companies.description:
Onlay is the first and only patented, end-to-end automation platform for the dental front office. Built by dentists who lived the day-to-day chaos and AI engineers who pioneered automation in healthcare billing.  Onlay was created inside OUR real practices to eliminate the bottlenecks holding dentistry back.

While others offer fragmented tools and partial fixes, Onlay is a complete solution. It verifies insurance, submits claims with the correct attachments, files secondary claims, posts payments, and resubmits denials all automatically, without human input.

It's not an add-on. It's your front office …fully automated.
Always on. 24/7. Even when your team's not.

With Onlay, you're setting the new standard in patient experience and operational excellence.

companies.name: Varick Agents
companies.description:
Varick Agents replaces manual coordination work at billion-dollar enterprises.

We deploy production agents into the tools you already run (Salesforce, NetSuite, ServiceNow, Workday) to close the work that breaks off-the-shelf automation: exception handling, cross-system reconciliation, judgment-based routing, Tier-2 tickets and claims, month-end close, audit-grade compliance logic.

What we ship:

Autonomous Financial Operations. Invoice processing, revenue reconciliation, month-end close, variance analysis, ledger updates with CFO-grade audit trails.

Resolution Agents. Read/write across the full stack (billing, auth, CRM, warehouse) to close Tier-2 tickets and claims without human intervention.

Forensic Data Pipelines. Messy PDFs, call logs, and emails converted into governed datasets with role-based access and observability.

Compliance Copilots. Policy-bound tools operating inside SOC 2, HIPAA, and bespoke regulatory environments. Every action logged, every action reversible.

How it runs:

The audit is non-negotiable. We map how operations actually move, not the SOP version.
